As he walked nearer, the bells grew in sound and seemed to call out to him. The ringing was so loud that it seemed to vibrate throughout the mighty land. The little boy followed the ringing, until he discovered a lake. There, in the middle of the lake was a large bell, perched atop a giant rock. He knew that this was were the ringing was coming from. The little boy ventured into the lake and swam out to the rock. When he reached it, he tolled the bell with all his might. He felt so proud, as the ringing echoed throughout the mighty land.

Little Sam had a very special treasure. It was a tiny necklace made of shiny stones. The necklace never left him, he always wore it around his neck. Every day, he put on his shiny treasure and went on an adventure. He played with his friends in the park and ran around the school grounds. He was never lonely. But one day, Little Sam had no one to play with. His friends were all busy, so he was all alone. He felt so lonely and sad. He looked at his necklace, and it suddenly seemed to twinkle brighter. It suddenly reminded him of good times with his friends, and it made him feel not so lonely anymore. Little Sam never went anywhere without his treasure again. He wore it everyday and felt safe and happy.

Timmy saw a hole in the ground and asked his Mommy what was in it. She told him she was going to bury something in it, so Timmy asked what it was. "It's a shield," Mommy said, "and I'm going to bury it so it stays safe for as long as possible." "Why do you want to bury it?" Timmy asked. "Because I'm worried it might get lost or stolen," Mommy said. Timmy helped Mommy bury the shield. They put it in the hole and covered it with dirt and then patted the dirt down with their hands. When they were done Timmy asked if the shield would be safe now. "Yes," Mommy said, smiling, "now the shield is safe and sound."

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She loved to sail on her boat with her mommy and daddy. They went to the lake and the sea. The boat was big and blue. Lily had a pretty blouse that she liked to wear. It was pink with flowers. One day, they went to the beach and Lily wanted to wear her blouse. But it was hidden in her bag! She looked and looked until she found it. They sailed on the sea and saw dolphins jumping in the water. Lily was happy and her blouse was pretty in the wind. When they got home, Lily was tired but happy from her fun day on the boat.

Once upon a time, there was a happy boy named Timmy. He loved playing with his computer. It was his favorite toy. One day, Timmy's computer stopped working. He felt sad and wanted to complain to his mom. "Mama, my computer is broken. I can't play with it anymore," Timmy said. His mom saw how sad he was and tried to cheer him up. She told him they could take the computer to the store to get it fixed. Timmy felt a little better and smiled. After a few days, they got the computer back and it was working again. Timmy was so happy and cheerful. He thanked his mom and played with his computer all day long. The end.

One day, Bob had a very important test. He was very nervous because he was not ready. He went to his friend, Mary, for help. He asked for an eraser. Mary was happy to lend him one. Bob was so proud. He thanked Mary and then ran off to take his test. During the test, he realized his eraser was helping him a lot. He was able to erase any mistakes he made and felt more confident. When he was done, he returned to Mary and gave her back the eraser. She was proud that Bob was able to use it to do well on his test. Bob was very thankful for his friend. She had lent him the eraser right in time. With it, he was able to do very well on his test.

Once upon a time, there was a wide, green meadow. In the meadow, there was a big, green tree. In that tree, there were lots of juicy grapes. A little girl went to the meadow. She could see all the grapes in the tree. The little girl wanted to catch a grape. She ran towards the tree and reached out her hand to grab one. She was too short to reach the grapes. The little girl wanted to catch the grape very much. She came up with an idea. She found a small box and put it under the tree. She stood on the box. Now she was tall enough to reach the grape. She grabbed it in her hand. The little girl was so happy! She had caught a big, juicy red grape. She was smiling as she ate it.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sally. She was only three years old, but she was very creative. She loved to design things. One day, Sally found a piece of fur in the garden. It was orange and fluffy. She picked it up and brought it inside to her room. Once in her room, Sally got to work. She began arranging the fur on her table, designing it into all sorts of wonderful shapes. Just then, her mom popped her head in to see what Sally was doing. "Oh Sally, what have you made?" she asked. "I'm designing!" Sally beamed proudly. Her mom smiled. "That's wonderful," she said. "Why don't you keep designing until dinner time." So Sally spent the rest of the afternoon designing with the orange fur, creating wonderful shapes, until she was called for dinner.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing dress-up and pretending to be different people. One day, she wanted to be a firefighter, but she didn't have a uniform. Lily went to her friend Max's house and asked if she could borrow his firefighter uniform. Max was happy to help and gave her the uniform. As Lily was walking home, she saw a big, scary dog. She was scared and didn't know what to do. Suddenly, a real firefighter came to her rescue and scared the dog away. "Thank you for scaring the dog away," Lily said to the firefighter. "You're welcome," he replied. "And that's a nice uniform you have there. Are you a firefighter too?" Lily smiled and said, "No, I just borrowed it from my friend Max." The firefighter laughed and said, "Well, you make a great pretend firefighter." Lily was happy and felt proud of her borrowed uniform.

George and Timmy were best friends. They did everything together. One day Timmy asked George if he wanted to make some popcorn. George thought it was a great idea. They got all the popcorn kernels and mixed them in a bowl. Then they put the bowl in the microwave and waited. Finally the popcorn was ready. It smelled delicious. But when George went to put some in a bowl, Timmy put his hand over the popcorn and said, "I want it all for myself." George was surprised. He told Timmy that it wasn't very nice to be so selfish. But Timmy didn't listen. So George and Timmy didn't share the popcorn. George was sad, but he knew he had done the right thing.

Once upon a time, a mum and her three year old daughter, Caroline, went for a walk. It was a windy day, and the leaves in the trees blew around in the wind. Caroline pointed up and said, â€œWhatâ€™s that?â€ Mum pointed to the top of a hill and said, â€œThatâ€™s the top of that big hill. Letâ€™s go up there, and see the view.â€ Caroline was excited to go. But when she got to the top of the hill, she felt a bit uncomfortable. It felt too high, and she didnâ€™t like the wind. Mum said, â€œCome on, love, itâ€™s okay. Weâ€™ll just look up here for a minute. You can see a lot of things from here.â€ Caroline reluctantly agreed. Together they looked all around, and saw a beautiful stretch of countryside. It included meadows, streams and hills, which made Caroline a bit less uncomfortable. Mum smiled and said, â€œSee, isnâ€™t this view lovely?â€ Caroline smiled back and said, â€œYes!â€

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to explore the world around her. One day, she found a tiny bug on the ground. She wanted to see it up close, so she asked her mom for her microscope. Her mom helped her set it up and Lily carefully put the bug under the microscope. She examined it closely and saw all the little details she couldn't see before. It was so cool! Later that day, Lily's mom gave her a cookie. But when she took a bite, she realized it was spoiled. She didn't like it at all and told her mom. Her mom said sorry and gave her a fresh cookie instead. Lily was happy again and went back to examining the world around her.

Once upon a time there was a nation. The nation was very obedient and followed the rules. All the people in the nation were happy and content. One day, the leader of the nation asked all the people to tie a special knot together. The knot was to stand for the nation's unity and strength. Everyone was excited about the idea and began to tie the knot. They all worked together to tie the knot and soon it was done. The knot was very strong and held the nation together. Everyone cheered with joy and thanked each other for helping out. From then on, the obedient nation always remembered the special knot that tied them together. They kept it safe and held on to it for always.

Once there was a little girl named Jill. She loved to explore and find fun things. One day she was playing in the garden and came across a funny knob. She twisted and twisted the knob, not knowing what it would do. Suddenly, her mom came out and saw her. She said, "Jill, what are you doing?" Jill replied, "I'm twisting the knob, Mommy. It's so fun!" Her mom smiled and said, "Well, it looks like that knob is connected to the hose. Let's see what happens when you twist it!" So, Jill twisted the knob and the hose began spraying water all over the yard! She laughed and ran around, playing in the wet grass. Her mom laughed too and declared that it was the funnest day ever!

Once upon a time, there was a farmer. He had many animals on his farm. He had cows, pigs, and chickens. The farmer was very nice to everyone. One day, a rude man came to the farm. The rude man wanted the farmer to give him some eggs. He did not say "please" or "thank you." The farmer did not like that. The farmer told the rude man, "You must request nicely for the eggs." The rude man thought about it and said, "Please may I have some eggs?" The farmer was happy and gave the rude man some eggs. The rude man learned to be nice when he asked for things. Everyone was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a happy boy named Tim. Tim loved playing with his colorful blocks. He would build tall towers and knock them down with a big smile on his face. One day, Tim felt his tummy rumble and he knew it was time for a snack. He ran to the kitchen and asked his mom for a cheerful sandwich. As he took a bite, he felt the ground tremble. Tim looked outside the window and saw a big truck driving by. The blocks on his table started to shake and Tim giggled with excitement.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a favorite jacket with a zipper that she loved to play with. One day, Lily went to the park with her mommy. She was playing on the swings when she noticed her jacket was missing. Lily looked everywhere but couldn't find her jacket. She started to cry because she loved that jacket very much. Suddenly, she saw a boy wearing her jacket! She went up to him and asked for her jacket back, but the boy said it was his. Lily didn't know what to do, so she went to her mommy. Her mommy asked the boy to part with the jacket and give it back to Lily. The boy finally agreed and gave the jacket back. Lily was so happy to have her favorite jacket back and hugged her mommy tightly, feeling quiet and content.

Once there was a goose who felt very excited. He wanted to go out and explore the world, so he began to fly. As he flew, he saw a bright and beautiful meadow below him. He was so excited that he couldn't resist and quickly descended to the ground. But, as soon as he landed, he noticed a cat. The cat was huge and fierce-looking. He was scared and tried to fly away, but the cat was too fast for him. The goose knew he had to surrender in order to survive. He held his wings close and stopped struggling. After a few moments, the cat tired of chasing the goose and walked away. The goose was relieved and stretched his wings to fly away. But before he left, he took a moment to appreciate the beauty of the meadow.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who loved to drink milk. She thought it was the best thing ever! One day she was walking around looking for something special. After a while she came across a bottle of luxury milk. It looked so yummy and delicious, she couldn't resist. So, she opened the bottle, put it to her lips and started drinking. But it was too strong and it hurt her throat. She started to cry and put the bottle down. She never drank anything like that again.

Jack and his mom were walking in the park when they saw an unknown bow lying on the ground. Jack loved bows, so he asked his mom if he could keep it. But his mom said no. She said it was dangerous because they didn't know who it belonged to. Jack was upset so he asked again. He pleaded, saying that it wasn't dangerous. His mom mentioned that it could have belonged to someone known or unknown. In the end, Jack was too stubborn and picked up the bow. No sooner had he touched it then he felt a sharp pain in his arm. When he looked back at the bow, he saw a needle sticking out of it. Jack ran to his mom in shock and pain. His mom was so worried she called 911. The needle had caused a serious infection, and Jack was taken to hospital. It was a bad ending for Jack and his mom, because the needle was a mystery to them and never known who put it there.

Once upon a time, there were two friends: Jack and Jill. Jack had a brilliant idea. He wanted to make something special using clay. He asked Jill for help to match the pieces of clay together. She was happy to join in and the two began to make something exciting. With Jill's help, shapes and designs began to appear from the clay. Hours passed, and eventually the friends had made something really fantastic. It was a beautiful clay castle. They stood back to admire their work. Jill said it was brilliant, and Jack was very proud. The castle was the perfect match for their creativity! They both knew it was something special. The end.

One day, two best friends were walking through the park. They saw a big tree and decided to lean against it. The taller one was curious and asked "why are we leaning on this tree?". The shorter one replied "because it feels good and it relaxes us". While they were leaning, the shorter one began to feel a little pain in his back. He asked the taller one, "did you feel the same pain?". The taller one leaned in and said, "No, I didn't feel any pain". He was curious to know why the smaller one felt pain, so the smaller one explained that they had been leaning on the tree too long and he was beginning to feel a bit stiff. The taller one thought that was a great idea, so they both moved away from the tree, and they felt much better. The moral of the story is to not stay in one position for too long or you may get a bit of pain.

Once upon a time, there were two twins called Tim and Tom. They loved to play together in the park. One day, they were running around and playing tag. Suddenly, Tim pointed to a big red trampoline and shouted, "Let's jump!". Tom and Tim both ran over to the trampoline. They jumped and jumped and jumped. Tom jumped high and long. Tim jumped lower and shorter. The twins laughed and shouted happily as they jumped around. They jumped in the air and around on the trampoline. Soon, they were jumping and jumping so much, they were both out of breath. Tom and Tim said goodbye and each went home. They were both so happy and they were looking forward to their next visit to the big red trampoline.

Once upon a time there was a little boy called Charlie. Charlie was three years old, and he loved to imagine the coolest things. One day, Charlie was in the kitchen with his mom. He saw one of the cabinets open, and he gasped with excitement. Inside was a mixer! Charlie said, "Mom, what is a mixer?" His mother replied, "It's a special machine that helps mix things together. What would you like to make with it?" Charlie thought for a few moments and then said, "Let's make something spicy!" His mother smiled and carefully opened a cupboard. She pulled out some onions, peppers, and some spices. Charlie imagined how delicious it would taste. His mother helped him put the ingredients into the mixer, and she pressed the button. The mixer began to whir and the ingredients all blended together. When the mixture was done, it looked and smelled great. Charlie and his mom tasted it, and it was the most delicious and spicy thing they'd ever eaten!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She was 3 years old and loved playing with all of her toys. One day, Lucy was playing with her dolls when her mommy gave her a special box. Lucy opened the box and inside was a label with a map and a key. She blinked in surprise. "What is this, Mommy?" She asked. "It's a surprise!" said her mommy excitedly. So with the help of her mommy, Lucy followed the map and used the key to open a door at the start of a path. Along the path, there were lots of useful things. Lucy found a bag of balls, a toy guitar and even a book. She was so delighted with her surprises! The little girl blinked once more in surprise at all the useful things she had found and smiled with glee.

Once upon a time, there was an old rabbit. He lived in the woods with many other animals. Every day, he wanted to learn something new. He saw the other animals scurrying around and noticed how much they knew. He wanted to be just like them! One day, he decided to learn how to climb the trees. He hopped to the base of the tree, but it was too high. He tried and tried, but he couldn't reach the top. He was starting to get frustrated. Then, a beautiful blue bird flew by. She noticed the old rabbit trying to climb the tree. She swooped down and offered to help. She flew to the branches above and told the old rabbit which way to jump. Finally, the old rabbit made it to the top of the tree. He was so excited! He could feel the wind in his fur and see the other animals below. He had learned a new trick! From then on, the old rabbit was never afraid to try something new.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to pick flowers in the meadow. One day, she saw a big blue flower that she wanted to pick. But the petals were harsh and prickly. Lily decided to gather other flowers instead. She found a bunch of pretty yellow ones and some soft pink ones too. She put them in a basket and took them home to her mom. Her mom was very happy and put the flowers in a vase. Lily felt proud that she could gather such beautiful flowers for her mom. She decided that next time she would be more careful and not try to pick the harsh blue flowers.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Bella. Bella was very modest and loved to play with scissors. One day she saw a pitch in the grass and wanted to cut it. So she ran over to it and grabbed her scissors and started cutting. As she cut it, a tiny fairy appeared! The fairy was very kind and told Bella that she wanted to give her a special gift. "What is the gift?", Bella asked. "I will give you a magical spell that will help you always make modest choices", the fairy said. Bella was excited and said, "thank you". She thanked the fairy and was so excited to use the magical spell. She was very happy and knew that now every time she made a decision, it would be a modest one.

One gloomy day, Jack and his dad were in the woods. Jack asked his dad, â€œWhat are we doing here?â€ His dad replied, â€œWe are going to hunt for some animals, son.â€ Jack asked, â€œWhat do we use?â€ His dad said, â€œWe use the gun.â€ Jack was excited to go on a hunting trip with his dad and they quickly got ready. Jackâ€™s dad loaded the gun and they set off into the woods. The trees looked dark and lonely and Jack shivered. After a while, they saw a deer drinking from a creek. Jackâ€™s dad told him to stay quiet as he took aim. Jack kept his eyes fixed on the deer as his dad took the gun and slowly pulled the trigger. BANG! The gunshot echoed, and the deer turned into a dumping sack, lifeless and still. Jack was horrified. He had never seen anything like that before. He felt sadness inside and a heavy sorrow hung over him like a dark cloud. He realized that hunting animals was wrong, and from that moment he decided never to do it again.

Once upon a time, there was a little rabbit named Benny. Benny was very enthusiastic about exploring the world around him. One day, Benny decided to go underground to see what was there. He hopped and hopped until he found a big hole. Benny went inside and saw many tunnels. He was so excited! Suddenly, Benny heard a voice. "Who goes there?" it said. Benny was scared, but he answered, "It's me, Benny the rabbit. Who are you?" The voice replied, "I'm a mole. What are you doing down here?" Benny explained that he wanted to explore. The mole was happy to have a new friend and showed Benny around. As they were walking, the ground started to shake. Benny was scared again, but the mole explained that it was just a train passing by underground. Benny was amazed! He had never seen a train before. They watched as the train shook the ground and then disappeared into the darkness. Benny thanked the mole for the adventure and hopped back up to the surface, excited to tell his friends about his underground journey.

Once upon a time, there was a small girl named Annie. Annie had a special toy, she called it her treasure. One day she was very brave and decided to explore the garden. Suddenly a big, mean dog appeared! Its teeth were sharp, making Annie tremble in fear. The dog had a nasty mean whip in its mouth. Annieâ€™s mom quickly ran outside and protected her daughter. Mom shouted at the dog, making it run away. Annie was so embarrassed that the dog had scared her. But Mom hugged Annie and told her that it was ok to be scared. From that day on, Annie was brave and never touched her treasure again!

Once upon a time there was a rat. His name was Peter and he was very brave. One day, Peter started to explore the world outside his house. He came across a bottle full of a strange liquid. Peter sniffed it and it smelled bad. He thought it was poison. Though he was scared, Peter decided to drink a drop. Suddenly, the poison tasted sweet! Peter was so surprised that he dropped the bottle and it broke. From the poison spilled out a rare gem. Peter was so excited that he started jumping up and down. A nearby rabbit watched him curiously. The rabbit asked, "What happened? Why are you so happy?" Peter smiled and said, "I found something very special. It's a rare gem that I found in the bottle of poison I drank!"

Once upon a time, there was a rare pear. It was a very special pear indeed. It was bigger than other pears, and it was also a beautiful golden yellow colour. One day, a little boy found the rare pear and he decided to take it home with him. He carefully attached it to his belt so he wouldn't lose it. The little boy took the special pear to his mum. His mum smiled and said that the pear was very special. She knew one way to make it even more special. So the mum peeled the pear, cut it into slices, and put it into a tasty fruit salad. They all ate the fruit salad and it was delicious! The little boy had found a rare pear, and he had made a special treat for everyone. What a wonderful day!

The family were out painting in the park in the winter. It was cold, but they were having fun. Dad had a pot of bright green paint. He asked little Toby, who was three, to help him paint. Toby replied, "Yes please!" Dad dipped a brush into the paint and handed it to Toby. Toby carefully painted a big circle on the ground. He said, "Look, Daddy. I'm making a big round smooth circle!" "That's great, Toby!" said Dad. Toby smiled and continued painting. As he painted, he laughed and sang, "I'm painting in the winter, I'm making it bright," he sang. When the painting was finished, Toby and Dad stood back and smiled. They looked at the bright, smooth circle in the winter park and they were both very proud.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a tooth that was wiggly and ready to come out. One day, Lily was playing with her friends when she tripped and fell. Her tooth fell out and was hidden in the grass. Lily's friend, Timmy, saw what happened and said, "Don't be embarrassed, Lily. Losing a tooth is normal." Lily felt better and together they searched for the hidden tooth. After a few minutes, they found it and Lily was so happy. She showed her tooth to everyone and they all cheered. From that day on, Lily was proud to have a missing tooth and she told everyone about how it happened. She was no longer embarrassed and felt happy to have such great friends.

Once upon a time, two good friends were playing in the park. They were very happy as they watched the birds in the blue sky. The first friend, a girl, told the boy: "I can do something special today." The boy asked: "What do you mean?" The girl replied, with a wink: "It will be a surprise!" The two of them continued to play in the park and soon they stumbled upon a beautiful flower garden. There were pretty pink roses and yellow sunflowers. The boy was amazed. The girl then said: "Remember my surprise! You will see soon." The boy wondered what the surprise could be and he asked her. But the girl just smiled and wouldn't tell him anything. Eventually, the two friends went home. When the girl opened her door, she showed him the surprise: she had brought a puppy home! It was a small and cute pup, with big eyes and a wagging tail. The boy was so happy and excited! He smiled, and even winked to the girl as he said: "This is the best surprise ever!"

Once upon a time there was a little girl who loved computers. She was so happy that she could store her favourite game on her computer. One day she decided to go to the store to buy a brand new computer that was even more great than her old one. She chose a red computer with a rainbow on the screen. She was so excited that she could play games on it and store them safely. At home the little girl saved all her games on her new computer and played them over and over again. She thought it was great and she wanted to show it to everyone. She ran to the kitchen to tell her mom that she got a new computer and was so proud of it. Her mom said "That's great! I'm so proud of you." The little girl smiled and hugged her computer, happy that she chose such a great one.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to draw with her pastel colors. One day, she felt dizzy because she played too much. Her mom told her to rest and unpack her toys. Lily started to unpack her toys and found her pastel colors. She wanted to draw a big sun and flowers. But she remembered that she was dizzy. She thought, "I should listen to mom and rest first." After resting, Lily felt better. She was not dizzy anymore. She unpacked her pastel colors and drew a beautiful picture. She learned that it is good to listen to mom and take care of herself.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play outside when the weather was nice. She liked to run and jump and play with her friends. One day, Lily was being a little bit clumsy and she fell down and scraped her knee. It hurt a lot and she started to cry. Lily's mommy came outside and picked her up. She told Lily that everything was going to be okay and that she needed to relax. They went inside and mommy put a band-aid on Lily's knee. After that, they sat down and watched the weather on TV. They saw that it was going to be sunny and warm for the rest of the day. Lily felt happy and she smiled. She was glad that she could relax and enjoy the nice weather with her mommy. The end.

Once upon a time there were two best friends, Mia and Bob. Mia wanted to do something interesting, so she decided to join a team. Mia asked Bob if he wanted to be on the team too. Bob was excited and said, â€œYes!â€ Mia and Bob got ready to join the team. They put on their special clothes, and bowed to the team leader to show their respect. The team leader was so impressed with them. The team did a lot of interesting activities together, like running, playing tag, and jumping rope. Mia and Bob had so much fun with the team that they could not stop laughing and smiling. At the end of the day, Mia and Bob bowed to the team leader to say goodbye. It was the most interesting day ever!

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Benny. Benny loved to eat carrots. One day, Benny saw a big carrot in the garden, but he couldn't reach it. He looked sad. A little girl named Lily saw Benny and asked, "What's wrong, Benny?" "I want that carrot, but it's too high," Benny said. "I promise to help you," Lily said. She picked up Benny and lifted him up so he could reach the carrot. Benny was so happy. "Thank you, Lily! You are so kind," he said. Suddenly, they heard a loud noise. It was Benny's mom calling him for dinner. Benny and Lily ran back to Benny's house to eat some yummy carrot soup. They laughed and played until it was time for bed. Benny was grateful for his friend Lily and promised to help her if she ever needed it.

Once upon a time, there was a tall giraffe named Jerry. Jerry loved to ride his bicycle and pedal around the jungle. One day, he saw a group of monkeys playing with a ball. Jerry wanted to join in, but the monkeys said he was too tall to play with them. Jerry felt sad and decided to ride his bicycle to cheer himself up. As he was riding, he saw a small bird stuck in a tree. The bird was chirping for help. Jerry knew he had to do something. He got off his bicycle and used his long legs to reach the bird. He gently picked up the bird and put it back on the branch. Just then, a mean crocodile appeared and tried to attack Jerry. But Jerry was quick and kicked the crocodile with his strong legs. The crocodile ran away, and the bird thanked Jerry for saving its life. From that day on, the monkeys realized that Jerry was a kind and brave giraffe, and they invited him to play with them. Jerry was happy and grateful for his bicycle and his long legs that helped him save the bird and make new friends.

Once upon a time there was a young girl named Lucy. She was out walking one day and she had an urge to go searching for something. She walked for miles and miles until she came across a deep and dark cave. She wanted to explore it so she stepped inside. The cave was something terrible. It was full of strange noises and Lucy couldn't see anything. Suddenly, a voice called out from the darkness: "Who are you?" Lucy was scared but she bravely shouted out: "I'm Lucy. Who are you?" The voice replied: "I am the King of the Cave, and you have come to search my kingdom. What do you seek?" Lucy thought for a moment and said: "I'm looking for adventure!" The King was pleased and he said: "Then come with me and I shall show you the way!" So with the King leading the way, Lucy began her exciting search.

Once upon a time, there was a fat cat. The fat cat lived in a small house. The house had a chimney. One day, the fat cat saw a thin dog. The thin dog looked sad. The fat cat wanted to help the thin dog. The fat cat said, "Let's add food to your bowl." The thin dog smiled and ate the food. The thin dog felt better. The fat cat and the thin dog became friends. The fat cat and the thin dog played together. They learned that sharing and helping each other made them happy. The fat cat and the thin dog lived happily ever after. The moral of the story is to be kind and help others.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl and her mom. The girl was named Cherry. One day, Cherry's mom asked her if she wanted to go for a ride. But Cherry refused. "Let's go for a ride, Cherry," said mom. "No, I refuse," said Cherry. Mom was feeling sad because Cherry didn't want to go on a ride. But then an idea came to her. "I know! Let's get a comfortable car seat for our ride," said Mom. Cherry smiled and said, "Yes! I like that." So mom got a comfortable car seat and put it in their car. Finally, Cherry and mom went for the ride. They both had a lot of fun. At the end of the ride they got an ice cream cone with a cherry on top. Cherry was so happy!

Once there was a little boy named Jack who wanted to help out on the farm. One day he decided to get some onions from the garden. He was so excited to help his mom and he wanted to do a great job. Jack grabbed a big bucket and loaded it with onions. It was hard work, but he was determined to fill the bucket. He was sweating and his hands were rough from the work. When he was almost done, he dropped the bucket and all the onions spilled out. He was so disappointed but his mom encouraged him to go back and start again. Jack worked hard to load the bucket with onions again. This time, he was very careful and he took his time to make sure he didn't make a mistake. At the end of the day, Jack was proud of himself. He learned that sometimes it's important to be careful when you do a task, especially if it's something important.

Once upon a time, there was an ordinary boy called Dave. Dave was like other boys, he liked playing and being silly. One day, Dave was playing in his garden with his friends. All of a sudden, he saw a trumpet, lying in the grass! Immediately, he picked it up and started to blow. At first, it sounded like a squeak but then a loud noise came out. Everyone was so surprised and happy! Dave kept on blowing, louder and louder, until the trumpet made a really loud sound! All his friends started to scream with laughter. Later on, Dave learned how to play the trumpet and it became his favourite thing to do. He played it all the time, even at bed time. He had lots of fun and everyone around him enjoyed it too. They loved his music and they all smiled whenever they heard it. The End.

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful girl named Lily. Lily liked to play with her toys and draw pictures. One day, Lily found a letter in the mailbox. She was very excited to see what was inside. Lily ran to her mom and said, "Mom, look! A letter! Can we open it?" Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, let's open it together." So, Lily and her mom sat down at the table to open the letter. Lily's mom took a pair of scissors and carefully cut the letter open. Inside, there was a picture of a big, happy sun. Lily laughed and said, "Wow, what a nice sun!" They put the picture on the fridge, and Lily felt very happy every time she saw the cheerful sun.

Once upon a time, there was a brave boy named John who lived in the woods. He was only three years old and he loved to explore. One day, he decided to go down the big slide in the woods. He was excited, but he was also a little worried. As he started to go down the slide, he heard a loud noise. He was scared and looked around to find out what made that noise. He saw a big, scary bear! The bear growled at him and John ran away. John was very scared, but he thought of a plan. He went back to the slide and the bear was still there. That time, John was brave and he made a lot of noise while sliding down. The bear got scared and ran away from the slide. John was proud of himself for being brave. He learned an important lesson: being brave and having a plan is much better than being worried.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Max. Max loved to play outside in the sun. One day, Max's mommy gave him a cooler to take with him. Max was so happy and felt lucky to have a cooler. Max went to the park with his cooler. He put some juice and snacks in it. His friend Jack came to play with him. Jack saw the cooler and asked Max what it was for. Max explained to Jack that it was to keep their drinks and snacks cold. They had a lot of fun playing in the park and eating their snacks from the cooler. Max was happy that he had his lucky cooler with him. When it was time to go home, Max packed up the cooler and said goodbye to Jack. Max couldn't wait to use his cooler again and show it to his other friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play with his toy airplane and pretend he was a pilot. One day, Timmy saw a man who looked like a pilot. But it was a fake pilot! The fake pilot wanted to take Timmy's toy airplane away. Timmy didn't want to give it up, so he hid it in his room. But the fake pilot kept peeking in his window, trying to find it. Timmy was scared and didn't know what to do. But then he had an idea. He put a note on his toy airplane that said "Property of Timmy, not for fake pilots." The fake pilot saw the note and left Timmy alone. Timmy was happy he didn't have to give up his toy airplane and could keep pretending to be a pilot. The end.

Jessie had a thin guitar. It made him happy to play it. Every day, he would strum the strings and pluck little tunes. One day, however, something strange happened. He felt a tickle in his nose. He paused his playing and then he sneezed! His guitar made a funny noise when he sneezed. He smiled and continued playing, with the guitar sounding slightly different after the sneeze. Jessie kept playing and eventually the strange sound went away. Jessie kept his guitar thin and kept playing delicious melodies every day.

Kate was walking her dog, Furball, in the park when he suddenly ran off. She knew where Furball liked to go and quickly followed. Sure enough, Furball was making a big mess inside the sand box. Kate yelled, "Furball, what are you doing?" Furball was digging a hole with his heel and making a big mess. Kate said sternly, "Furball, you need to stop right now!" Furball looked ashamed and sat on his hind legs. Kate took Furball's leash and attached it around his collar. She said, "We need to go home now. No more digging holes with your heel!" Furball hung his head low and walked home. Kate felt ashamed of him.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved going to the store with his mommy. One day, they went to the store to buy some apples. They walked down the aisle and Timmy saw an impressive toy car. He said, "Mommy, can we buy that car?" Mommy said, "Not today, Timmy. Maybe next time." As they walked to the checkout, Timmy noticed his zipper was down. He said, "Mommy, can you help me with my zip?" Mommy smiled and said, "Sure, Timmy." She zipped up his jacket and they paid for the apples. Timmy was happy to have his zipper fixed and excited for the next time they would go to the store to buy something impressive.

Once there was a little girl named Lucy. She was so happy! She had two big eyes that blinked in the sunlight. Lucy was exploring and found a cool, new place. It was a strange point, a tall and green one. She wanted to explore it and learn about it. She was a bit scared but she gathered her courage and climbed up the point. Once up there, she looked around and saw the most beautiful view. She blinked, feeling safe and content. It was the best surprise! She was so excited and rewarded for her brave climb. She wanted to tell her family about it and come back soon! From that day on, the point was her favorite place to visit.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to draw and paint. One day, her mommy bought her a big blackboard to display her art. Lily was very happy and started drawing right away. But one day, Lily's little brother came and scribbled all over her beautiful drawing. Lily was very upset and started crying. Her mommy came and saw what happened. She told Lily's brother that he should be sorry for what he did. Lily's brother felt bad and said sorry to Lily. Lily forgave him and they both started drawing together on the blackboard. They learned that it's important to be kind to each other and respect other people's things. From that day on, they always shared the blackboard and drew together happily ever after.

Once upon a time there was a big, round thing called Earth. It was very interesting and included lots of things. The Earth had green grass, bright blue sky, fluffy clouds, and naughty bugs. There were big birds that flew high in the sky and funny fish that swam in the oceans. The Earth had people who lived on it too. Some people had beds to sleep in, and some people had sweaters to keep them warm. Some people ate ice cream, and some people ate pizza. The Earth was a very special place with lots of things for people to see and do. From time to time people visited the Earth from other places and joined in the fun. The Earth was full of interesting things and everyone liked it very much.

Once there were two friends. One was called Bob and the other was called Alex. They were always playing together. One day, Bob wanted to do something different and asked Alex to join him. So, they decided to get a tutor. The tutor was very nice and was very competitive. They began to do their lessons with the tutor. Every day, something exciting would happen! They would learn new words, practice their maths and lots of other games too. Bob and Alex had great fun with their tutor. They couldn't wait to see what would happen next!

One day, Jill and Mum were taking a walk by the park. Mum stopped by the big green bin and said to Jill: â€œLetâ€™s pick up all the litter on the ground and put it in the bin, okay?â€ Jill grinned and promised to do it. She was so proud of helping to keep her favourite park clean. Jill then started running around, picking up pieces of rubbish and putting them in the bin. Mum watched, smiling. When Jill finished, Mum gave her a hug and said: â€œWell done, Jill! I'm so proud of you. Youâ€™ve done a great job.â€ Jill smiled. She felt very proud. Together, they went home and made some delicious hot chocolate to celebrate.

Once there was a wide napkin lying on the kitchen floor. It had never been used before. Everyone in the house wanted to know where it had come from. Mum decided to ask the 3 year old. He had been in the kitchen moments before. He told her he had found it and wanted to do something special with it. Mum smiled and suggested they have a picnic right there in the kitchen. She helped him to fill the napkin with crackers, cheese and juice. They finished eating and had lots of fun. At the end of the picnic they made sure to put the napkin away neatly. That way they could find it again next time they wanted to have a picnic in the kitchen.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was very hungry. He went to his mom and said, "Mom, I am hungry. Can I have some food?" His mom smiled and said, "Yes, Tim. Let's go to the kitchen and find something to eat." They went to the kitchen and Tim's mom asked him to kneel down by the cupboard. Tim kneeled down and opened the cupboard. Inside the cupboard, there were many snacks. Tim saw cookies, chips, and fruits. He looked at his mom and asked, "What is the truth, Mom? Can I eat all of these snacks?" His mom laughed and said, "No, Tim. You can only pick one snack. Eating too many will make your tummy hurt." Tim thought for a moment and picked an apple. He was happy with his choice and ate the apple with a big smile on his face.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to write stories and draw pictures. One day, she went for a walk with her mom and saw something unusual. It was a big purple bird! Lily: "Mommy, look! An unusual bird!" Mom: "Wow, that's quite a sight! Do you want to write a story about it?" Lily: "Yes, I will write a story about the big purple bird with my crayons." So, when they got home, Lily sat down and wrote a story about the big purple bird. She drew pictures of it too. Her mom read the story and said, "You have a great imagination, Lily!" And Lily smiled because she was happy that she could write stories and draw pictures.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat blueberries. One day, she went to the park with her mommy and saw a blueberry bush. She picked some blueberries and ate them. They were very yummy. Suddenly, her friend Timmy came to the park. He had a thoughtful look on his face. "What's wrong?" asked Lily. "It's my birthday today, but I don't have anyone to celebrate with," said Timmy. Lily had an idea. "Let's celebrate your birthday together!" she said. She gave Timmy some blueberries and they had a little party in the park. Timmy was so happy and grateful for his thoughtful friend.

The windy day was quiet and still. Little Timmy decided to take a walk. As he walked he found something smelly. It was a box! Curious, he opened it to explore. Suddenly a giant dragon flew out of the box! Timmy was so scared, he stood there and couldn't move. "RRAAAAAAAAWR!" The dragon bellowed. "I am here to strike!" Timmy was so scared, he didn't know what to do. Suddenly he heard a wise old voice. It said, "Do not be afraid, Timmy. Strike the dragon with your courage." So Timmy gathered up all his courage and shouted, "No! You will not strike me!" The dragon was so surprised. He flew away, never to be seen again. The windy day was quiet again and Timmy felt proud of his bravery.

Once there was a little girl. She was very happy. She had a good swing in her garden. She loved to play on it. Every day she would cover herself with a blanket and swing as high as she could. One day, a strong wind blew and made her swing go higher and higher. She went so high that she could see past the trees in her garden. She kept going until she could see the sun in the sky. She was so happy and shouted, â€œLook at me! I'm so high!â€ When she had had enough, the little girl came back down to the ground. She used the blanket to cover herself up and snuggled in for a good nap. She had a big smile on her face as she dreamt of all the fun she had had on her swing.

Once there was a girl named Joy. She was three years old and liked to explore. There was one thing she liked to do more than anything, and that was to reach for the stars. The other day, Joy looked around her room and saw something she had never seen before. It was a funny looking notebook with an open cover. It was so colourful and fun! She grabbed it quickly and opened it up. Suddenly, a voice said, "What do you want?" It was coming from inside the notebook. Joy was so surprised! "I want to reach the star!", said Joy. The voice answered, "Well, Joy, this notebook can help you reach your dream. Turn the page and you'll see how." Joy turned the page and there was a map with a star right in the middle. Joy was so excited! She followed the map, and sure enough, soon she was standing right beneath a star. Joy had reached her dream!

Once upon a time, there was an adorable little cat named Kitty. She loved to play with her toys and take naps in the sun. One day, Kitty saw a big bowl of spaghetti on the table. She thought it looked yummy and wanted to take a bite. Kitty tried to jump on the table, but she was too small. She had an idea. She would take a chair and push it near the table. Kitty worked hard to push the chair. She was getting closer and closer to the spaghetti. Finally, the chair was close enough. Kitty jumped on the chair, then onto the table. She could now reach the spaghetti. Kitty took a big bite and it was so yummy! She was very happy that she could get the spaghetti all by herself.

One day, a fish was swimming in the deep sea. He liked to look at the bright colors of the coral and the plants. He was happy and curious. He saw a big shadow above him. He swam up to meet it. He thought it was a friend. But it was a shark! The shark had sharp teeth and a hungry look. The fish was scared and tried to swim away. But the shark was faster and chased him. The fish saw a small hole in a rock. He swam inside and hid. The shark could not fit in the hole. He was angry and went away. The fish was safe, but he learned a lesson. He learned that not everyone he meets is a friend. He learned that he should be careful and not go too close to strangers. He learned that the deep sea can be dangerous. He was glad that he had a safe home in the rock. He thanked the rock for saving him.

Once there was a little fish who lived in the sea. He was having a very nice day and was swimming around looking for food, when suddenly he saw a hook! He was so scared, he quickly hid behind a rock. The fish was very uncomfortable. He wanted to stay away from the hook, but he was also very curious. He wanted to know what it was. So he decided to move a little closer to take a look. He moved slowly and carefully towards the hook, and he could see that it was there to catch fish. He had to find a way to stay away and stay safe. So the fish had an idea. He saw that there were some weeds near the hook. He swam over and hid underneath them. Now the fish felt much more comfortable. He was hidden underneath the weeds, and he could stay away from the hook. He was safe and happy now.

Once there was a girl called Jane. She was very polite and kind. One day, she wanted to help her Mom in the kitchen. She went to the cupboard and found a plate. She was so excited and carried it to the kitchen table. Suddenly, Jane heard her Mom calling her, "Jane honey, could you be a polite girl and carry the plate to the dining room?" Jane smiled and said, "Yes Mommy, I can do that". She neatly carried the plate to the dining room, and put it on the table. Jane's Mom hugged her and said, "Thank you sweetheart, you are such a good girl!" Jane was so pleased and could not stop smiling.

Once upon a time, there was a girl called Jane. She was three years old and she loved to explore the world. One day she decided to take a step into the garden. She suddenly felt dizzy, she started to blink and wobble about. "What's wrong Jane?" asked Mummy. "I feel so dizzy!" cried Jane. Mummy and Jane looked around the garden. There were lots of colourful flowers and buzzy bees. "Let's sit down on the grass," said Mummy. They sat next to each other and looked up at the cloudy sky. The clouds slowly moved, making everything seem very calm. Jane started to feel better, her dizzy feeling had gone away. Mummy smiled and asked, "Feeling better now?" Jane smiled back and nodded. "Let's go and explore," said Mummy. And off they went, taking small steps together, into the wonderful garden.

The park was full of noisy color. Everywhere the little girl looked, there were bright blues, reds and yellows. She smiled wide as she twirled around, taking in all the happy sights. Suddenly, she heard a sound, and a friendly voice. â€œHello little one!â€ the voice said. â€œAre you ready to clap?â€ The girl looked all around, puzzled. She loved to clap, and she didnâ€™t know anything about this game. â€œCome on,â€ the voice said. â€œLetâ€™s see how loud you can clap!â€ The girl smiled even wider. She raised her hands and let out a loud clap. The voice said â€œWell done! Now, can you do it even louder?â€ The girl tried her best and clapped even bigger. To her surprise, a parade of people suddenly came running around the corner. They were dressed in all the colors she had seen earlier! Some were spinning, and some were dancing. The little girl smiled, wide, and broke into applause. The crowd clapped back, and it was the noisiest, brightest thing she had ever seen.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Jessie. She was three years old and loved to paint. She had all sorts of colors, but her favorite was pink. One day, she took her paintbrush and some pink paint, ran to a beautiful open field and started to paint. She painted swirls and shapes in the sky, making them look graceful and pretty. Suddenly, she heard a voice behind her. It was her mom! She said, "What are you doing, Jessie?" Jessie replied, "I'm painting in the sky! It's so fun!" Her mom smiled and said, "It looks lovely! You are so graceful at painting." Jessie beamed with joy, and finished her painting. With the pink paint, she had managed to make something so beautiful that it even made her mom smile.

Once there was a family that lived in a house had a big garden. Every day the little boy and his parents would go outside and play in the garden. One day, the little boy looked around and said, "I dare you to climb up the tree!" His parents laughed and said, "That's a big dare!" So the little boy bravely climbed up the tree and when he reached the top he shouted, "I did it!" His parents clapped and cheered. The little boy was so happy that he ran around the garden, laughing and jumping. His parents chased him around and laughed too. It was a happy day for the family and ever since, the boy and his family have been returning to the garden to explore and play.

Jimmy and Lily were best friends. They did everything together. One day, they decided to go for a walk in the park. When they arrived, they met a beautiful butterfly. Jimmy and Lily were so excited that they wanted to show their friends. They asked the butterfly to follow them and the butterfly said yes. The result of their visit was even better than expected. Everybody in the park was amazed to see the butterfly, and everyone wanted to take pictures. Jimmy and Lily were so proud and grateful for the chance to meet such a beautiful creature. They thanked the butterfly for its time and sent it on its way. Jimmy and Lily said goodbye and waved as the butterfly flew away. They were so happy to have this wonderful experience. And they would never forget the amazing result of their meeting.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max had a red ball that he loved to play with. One day, Max saw a thief take his red ball. Max was sad and angry. He wanted his red ball back. Max chased the thief to an empty house. The thief tried to hide the red ball, but Max was smart. Max knew he needed to succeed in getting his red ball back. Max looked everywhere in the empty house. He looked under the bed, behind the door, and in the closet. Finally, Max found the red ball in a box. Max was very happy. He succeeded in getting his red ball back from the thief. Max ran back home with his red ball. From that day on, Max played with his red ball and made sure to keep it safe.

Once there was a mouse. The mouse liked to lean against the wall. It was all alone, until one day, it found a fake friend. The fake friend was a toy mouse. It was so clever that it leaned against the wall just like the real mouse. The two mice became friends, leaning against the wall together. They chatted away happily, enjoying their new found friendship. One day, a little girl saw the two mice leaning together. She was so excited! She picked them up and put them into her pocket, then took them home. The little girl loved when the mice leaned against each other, so she put them back in the same position and they continued to be best friends. The little girl was very happy. The real mouse and the fake mouse were very happy too, leaning together side by side.

One day, Molly was walking in the park. She wanted to have fun. She sat on the grass and started to gaze around. The sky was a beautiful gray. In the distance, Molly saw a tall clock tower. It was so big and impressive. She got up and started walking towards it. Molly arrived at the clock tower and looked up. She could see all the way to the top. It was a very beautiful thing. She began counting the clock's hands. She could see that it was almost noon. Molly continued to gaze in wonder at the clock tower. She felt so small standing in front of it. With a big smile on her face, she waved at the clock and said goodbye. She had had a wonderful time.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her phone, talking to her grandma and playing games. One day, Lily's phone stopped working. She tried to turn it on, but it wouldn't work. Lily was very sad. Her kind mom saw her daughter's sadness and promised to fix the phone. Lily was happy and waited patiently. A few days later, her mom gave her a new phone. Lily was overjoyed and thanked her mom with a big hug. From that day on, Lily was careful with her phone and made sure to turn it off when she wasn't using it. She was happy to have a kind mom who always looked out for her.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Ann. She loved to travel with her mom and dad. One day, they went to a big park. It was a cold day, so Ann wore a warm coat. In the park, they found a strawberry bush. The strawberries were big and red. Ann picked some strawberries with her mom and dad. They were very happy. They went home and ate the strawberries. The strawberries were sweet and yummy. Ann loved the cold day at the park. She wanted to travel and find more strawberries with her mom and dad.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Lola. Lola was very hungry so she went to find something to eat. She knew there was some rice in the cupboard so she decided to go and get it. When Lola opened the cupboard, she found a big bowl of white rice. She took it out and put it on the table. Then she got a spoon from the drawer and began to eat. But as she was eating, it became very quiet. Lola looked around, but she couldn't see anyone. Then she heard a noise coming from outside the window. When she looked out, she saw a bird sitting on the branch of a nearby tree. The bird was chirping its beautiful song. Lola stopped eating and just listened. The bird's song made her feel very happy and she decided to sing along with it. She sang a quiet song and the bird sang with her. When she finished singing, Lola took one last scoop of the rice before putting the bowl away. Then she went back to playing with her toys, feeling very happy.

Once upon a time, a nosy king lived in a castle. He was nosy, because he liked to look out of the window to see what was happening all around. One day when he looked out, the king saw that the door to the castle was open! He was worried that something might get in, so he hurried over to close it. He reached the door and he closed it. Now the castle was safe and nothing could get in. The king then returned to his window to keep an eye on things, but this time he made sure that the door stayed closed.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside near the river. One day, Lily saw a big raft floating down the river. It looked so fun to play on! But Lily didn't have a raft of her own. She asked her friend if she could borrow their raft. Her friend said yes and Lily was so happy! Lily got on the raft and it was very comfortable. She floated down the river and saw so many pretty things. When she was done, she returned the raft to her friend and said thank you. From that day on, Lily loved borrowing things from her friends and going on adventures.

"What's in the sack, Mama?" asked Billy, a three-year-old boy. "It's a tie," she smiled and replied. "What's a tie, Mama?" "It's something special I wear around my neck. You can wear ties too when you grow up," she said. Billy smiled and touched the sack, feeling the soft fabric. He opened the sack and took out the tie. It was gray with a yellow stripe. "It looks bitter," he said. Mama laughed and said, "No, it's not bitter. It's called a pattern. It's cool, isn't it?" Billy nodded and put the tie back in the sack. He was excited to wear a tie when he was older.

Once, there was a little girl named Emma. She was three years old and loved to play hide and seek. One day she went to hide, but couldn't remember her number. She tried counting to 10 but then she got stuck. She sat on the stairs and started to miss her number. Then grandma came over and heard Emma's story. She saw that Emma was sad, so she told her to look very carefully. Grandma said, "Look around and be clear. What number do you see?" Emma looked around and there it was! Her number! She was so happy and she went out to play with her friends. Grandma was very proud of Emma for looking so clearly and she smiled. Emma never forgot her number again!

Little Sue was so excited. She was going to visit the hill today. As she packed her picnic basket, the sun came out in the sky. She put on her sun hat and skipped outside ready to explore the hill. As she started to climb the hill, it got steeper and steeper. She stopped and looked up. It looked like it was a long way to the top. Sue hesitated for a moment, but then decided she was ready for the challenge. But little did Sue know, as she climbed higher and higher, the hill got higher and higher. She had to stop and take a break. She looked down - it was very far to the bottom of the hill. She started to panic. Just then, she noticed a smooth path that curved around the top of the hill. She smiled and took a deep breath. She continued to climb and minutes later, she was at the top. She was so happy and proud of herself! She had climbed the hill!

Mummy and Daddy were in a hurry. They were getting ready to take their daughter, Lucy, to the zoo. Mummy said, "Hurry up, Lucy! We have to leave now. Shall we go get your coat?". Lucy said, "No, I don't want to go". Mummy said, "Why don't you want to go to the zoo?" Lucy said, "I'm scared of the dark. The zoo is dark and I don't want to go". Mummy said, "It's not dark, it's light. Just look out the window, you can see the sun shining". Lucy used her clever brain and thought about it. She looked out the window and saw that it was light outside. She smiled and said, "Okay, I'm ready to go". So Mummy, Daddy and Lucy all hurried to the zoo and had a lovely day.

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful bird named Bobo. Bobo had many pretty feathers. He liked to play and sing with his friends in the big tree. One day, Bobo saw a shiny thing on the ground. He wanted to give it to his friend as a gift. But to get the shiny thing, he had to lose one feather. Bobo thought it would be okay to lose just one feather. Bobo pulled out one of his pretty feathers and got the shiny thing. He gave it to his friend, and his friend was very happy. But soon, Bobo started to feel cold without his feather. He was sad and regretted losing his pretty feather. Bobo's friends saw that he was sad and cold. They all worked together to find a warm nest for Bobo. When they found the warm nest, Bobo was happy again. He learned that sometimes it's better to keep what you have, even if you want to make others happy.

Jane was a poor little girl. Her family did not have much money and her old home was quite small. She lived with her mom and dad in one room. One day, Jane saw that the window in their room was open. She was excited and ran over, wanting to see what was outside. When Jane saw the street below, she decided to slide down the window. Her mom shouted at her to stop, but Jane would not listen. She kept going further and further down the window. Her mom was so worried because Jane was going to get hurt. Suddenly, her dad's strong hands pulled Jane up from the window! He explained that she could not do this because it was so dangerous. Poor Jane had to stay inside the room but she promised to not slide down the window ever again.

Once there was a bunny. He was very excited because he wanted to find a new home. He hopped and hopped until he reached the ground. It was perfect! He wanted to make it his home. He started digging and digging until he had a hole. He looked around and decided he needed some leaves and twigs. Soon he had a cozy little house. Now he was ready to start his life in his new home. He was so excited! He was finally going to have the home he always needed.

Once upon a time, there was a little bee named Buzz. Buzz lived in a hive with his friends. One day, Buzz wanted to explore the world outside of the hive. His friend, Buzzy, warned him to be careful because there were fierce animals outside. Buzz stepped outside and saw a big flower. He flew towards it to collect some nectar. Suddenly, he saw a big, fierce bird flying towards him. Buzz quickly flew back to the hive and told Buzzy what happened. Buzzy said, "I told you it's dangerous outside, Buzz. You need to be careful." Buzz realized that Buzzy was right and promised to be more careful in the future. From that day on, Buzz stayed inside the hive and never ventured outside again.

Ben and Mia are friends. They like to play with blocks. They build a big tower with red and blue and yellow blocks. They are very proud of their tower. "Look, our tower is so high!" Ben says. "Yes, it is. We are good builders!" Mia says. They want to show their tower to their mom and dad. But they have to be quiet. Mom and dad are working in the other room. They do not like noise. "Let's make a promise," Ben says. "We will not knock down our tower. We will not shout or run. We will be quiet until mom and dad finish working." "OK, I promise," Mia says. "We will be quiet and careful." They hold hands and nod. They are good friends. They keep their promise. They wait for mom and dad to come and see their tower. They smile and whisper. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Jane. She was very excited because she was going to the park that day. When Jane arrived at the park, she saw a big, long bench. She ran to it and said "I want to load the bench!" She hopped on the bench and sat down. Then, a smart, little bird flew down and sat on the bench too. Jane was very surprised and started giggling. She and the bird talked and laughed until the sun set. Jane had so much fun that day. She loaded the bench with wonderful memories of her adventure at the park. She was so excited to have made a new feathered friend!

Once upon a time, there was a deaf shark. She liked to swim and bounce around in the ocean. One day, she saw a little fish with big red eyes. The little fish said to the shark, "Will you play with me?" The shark happily said yes and together they started to bounce in the sea. First, the fish and the shark bounced around big rocks. Then, the shark leapt over the fish and made a big splash. The little fish laughed and clapped his fins. Next, the shark raced the fish to the coral reef. She was so fast she beat the fish and reached the reef first! Finally, the shark and the fish swam together for hours. They were having so much fun! After a while, the sun started to set and the sky turned blue and pink. The fish and shark said goodbye and the shark swam back home. And they all lived happily ever after.

Wilma had her favourite trophy. It sparkled and made her feel special. Wilma always kept it close by, wherever she went. One day, Wilma tripped and the trophy fell to the ground. It made a loud, scary sound when it hit the ground. Wilma looked down and saw the trophy was all broken. Wilma felt a big sad in her belly. Her eyes welled up and soon enough tears came streaming down her cheeks. She missed her trophy so much. But then, something special happened. Wilma's daddy put the broken trophy back together with super glue. Wilma was so excited! She hugged her dad tight and ran off to show everyone her fixed trophy. This time Wilma was sure to keep it safe and sound. She was never going to miss it again.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to invite his friends over to play with his toys. One day, Timmy's dad decided to grill some burgers for Timmy and his friends. Timmy was excited to eat the yummy burgers. But then, Timmy's friend Billy did a stupid thing. He climbed onto the grill and got burned. Timmy's dad had to call an ambulance and Billy had to go to the hospital. Timmy felt very sad and scared. He learned that it's important to be careful around hot things like grills.

Once upon a time, there was a hungry little girl. She went to the kitchen and saw a mixer. The mixer had many buttons and knobs that the girl wanted to explore. So she climbed up onto a stool and sat down on the mixer. She twirled the knobs and pushed the buttons. She made many different kinds of sounds with the mixer. The girl soon got tired. She jumped off the seat, onto the floor. Next, she opened the fridge and found her favorite treat. She took the treat over to the table and sat down to enjoy it. She was so happy that she was no longer hungry. She thanked the mixer for all the fun it provided her!

Once upon a time, in a big park, there was a little ant named Tim. Tim was a fearful ant. He was scared of everything. One day, Tim went out to find food for his family. Tim saw a big tree with lots of shade. He thought it was a good place to rest. Under the tree, Tim found a pinch of food. He was so happy! But then, he saw a big bug. The big bug wanted to pinch Tim. Tim was very scared, but he had an idea. He ran into the shade and hid behind a leaf. The big bug could not find Tim. Tim felt safe in the shade. After the big bug left, Tim took the food and went back to his family. Tim was a brave ant now.

Once there was a little girl who was very eager to learn more. Every day she was very curious, asking lots of questions. One day, she noticed there was a bell on the wall and asked her mom what it was for. Her mom told her that if she was ever in trouble, or if she needed help, she should ring the bell. The little girl nodded and smiled, feeling safe knowing the bell was there. She was happy that her mom cared about her so much. The little girl never had to ring the bell, but it made her feel safe to know it was there. She knew that if she ever did need help, her mom cared enough to be there for her.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the park. One day, she saw a big yellow goose walking in the grass. Lily wanted to say hello to the goose, so she took a step closer. The goose looked at her with its big eyes and Lily felt a little scared. But then, the goose started to waddle away. Lily took another step and followed the goose. She watched it until it disappeared into the trees. Lily was happy to see the yellow goose and she went home with a smile on her face. From that day on, she would always look for the yellow goose whenever she went to the park.

Once upon a time there was a brave man. He had interesting pants and his friends called him Super Sam. One day Super Sam heard a loud noise. He went to see what it was and he saw a little kitten stuck in a tree! The little kitten meowed and Super Sam knew he must save it. He looked for a ladder and found one in the garden shed. He put on the interesting pants and climbed high up the ladder. He reached out and held the kitten gently in his hands. He carefully got back down the ladder and placed the little kitten on the ground. The little kitty thanked Super Sam and ran off. Super Sam was so happy he'd been able to save the little kitten. He was proud of his interesting pants and happy he was able to help!

Once upon a time, there was a big dragon named Timmy. Timmy loved to fly around and play with his friends. One day, Timmy and his friends were flying east when they saw a big castle. Timmy said, "Wow, that castle looks amazing! Let's go check it out!" As they got closer, they saw that the castle was being destroyed by a big storm. Timmy's friend, Sally, said, "Oh no, we have to help them!" So, Timmy and his friends used their dragon fire to help put out the fire and save the castle. The people in the castle were very happy and thanked Timmy and his friends for their help. From that day on, Timmy and his friends were known as the amazing dragons who saved the castle from being destroyed.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Allie. Allie had a special gift: whenever she was in a sunny field, something wonderful happened. One day, Allie was walking in a bright field and saw a little bird hurt and alone. She bent down and asked him why he was so sad. The bird replied, â€œI was flying high up in the sky when something terrible happened. I made a mistake and accidentally hurt someone I love. I am so sorry, but I donâ€™t think they will forgive me.â€ Allie smiled and said, â€œIf you love them and youâ€™re sorry, donâ€™t worry. They will forgive you.â€ The bird smiled back and flew away. Then, a special magical thing happened in the field: the sun broke through the clouds and the field was shining bright. Allie was happy knowing that love and forgiveness can make everything better.

Once upon a time, there was a brave little girl named Lily. She loved watching the sunset every day. One day, Lily was playing in the park when she saw a lost puppy. The puppy was scared and alone. Lily knew she had to help the puppy. She called her mommy and daddy for help. They came right away and together they took care of the puppy. Lily felt happy that she was able to help the puppy. Lily learned that being brave means helping others when they need it. She also learned that calling for help is important when you need it. From that day on, Lily always looked out for others and called for help when she needed it.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Mia. She loved to collect things. Mia wanted to collect a sheet because she loved playing with cloths. She asked her Mama if she could have one. Mama said yes and brought Mia a very pretty sheet. It was made of soft, smooth fabric and light blue in colour. One sunny day, Mia and her Mama went into the garden to collect some flowers. Mia found lots of pretty flowers that she wanted to pick. But when she bent down to smell a flower, something surprised her. It tasted very bitter! Mia became sad because she didn't understand why the flower was bitter. Her Mama smiled and said it was because of the rain which made the flowers taste like that. Mia was happy now. She put her pretty sheet down, and lay on it while they continued to look at the flowers. She also made a promise to never forget how a bitter flower smells.

Once there was a weird bin. It was red and it rocked back and forth all the time. Every day the bin rolled around, rocking back and forth. One day, it rolled right up to a little boy. The little boy thought it was weird, and he wanted to find out what was inside it. He looked inside and saw a big surprise - toys! There were all kinds of toys in the bin, just waiting for him. He was so excited! He began collecting the toys and putting them in a pile. There were dolls and cars, and a little stuffed dog. All the toys looked so much fun! The boy played with the toys all afternoon. He took the doll for a walk and rode the cars around. He even made the stuffed dog bark. It was so much fun! When it was time to go home, the little boy put the toys carefully back in the bin and said goodbye. The bin rocked and rolled away, just like always. The little boy waved, and smiled, knowing that he had found a special friend.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Teddy and Lily. They liked playing together. Today, the two friends wanted to play a game. They had to choose what to do. Lily said: "Let's try a hide and seek today. It will be fun!" But Teddy wasn't so sure. He replied: "No, I'm feeling a bit sleepy today. Maybe another day." Lily thought for a moment then suggested: "How about a colouring game? We can draw and colour in the day!" Teddy smiled. He liked the idea. "Ok, let's go!" he said. So off they went and had lots of fun colouring in the day. Teddy was no longer sleepy. When they finished, Lily laughed and said: "Let's do this again tomorrow!" Teddy agreed and the two friends smiled, excited for the next game they would choose.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in her garden and look for worms. One day, she found a worm that was very kind. "Hello, little worm," said Lily. "Can you explain to me why you like to live in the dirt?" The worm wriggled and replied, "I like the dirt because it's cozy and safe. And there's always yummy things to eat!" Lily smiled and said, "That's very interesting. You're such a kind worm. Would you like to come play with me?" The worm happily agreed and they played together all afternoon. From that day on, Lily always looked for her kind worm friend whenever she played in the garden.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and explore the world around her. One day, she was walking in the park with her mom when she saw a pond with water in it. Lily said, "Mommy, can I play in the water?" Her mom replied, "I don't think that's a good idea, Lily. The water might not be clean." Lily looked at the water and it seemed dirty. She said, "The water doesn't seem available to play in." Her mom smiled and said, "That's right, Lily. We'll find another place to play where the water is clean and safe." Lily was happy to hear that and they continued their walk in the park, looking for a better place to play.

Jimbo was wandering through the woods. He saw something bright. It was a ball of yarn. Jimbo reached his hands out and touched it. He liked the way it felt. It was soft and fluffy. He picked it up and held it close to his chest. Jimbo began to wander through the forest again. He played with the yarn in his hands. He was careful not to drop it. He wanted to keep the yarn safe. He made it back to his house and he put his new treasure away in a special box that he kept in his bedroom. Every day, he would take it out and play with it before bedtime. He was so happy to have found this bright yarn.

Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Lily. She loved to eat rice for dinner every day. She would always ask her mommy to make her some rice with vegetables. One day, Lily went to the park to play with her friends. She saw a young boy who was crying because he lost his toy. Lily asked him what happened and the boy told her he couldn't find his favorite toy car. Lily felt sad for the young boy and wanted to help him. She looked around and found the toy car near a tree. The young boy was very happy and thanked Lily. From that day on, they became good friends and would often play together at the park. Lily loved having a new friend and was happy to help him find his toy car.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys in the kitchen while her mom cooked. One day, Lily's tummy started to rumble and she felt very hungry. "Mommy, I'm hungry!" Lily said. "I know, sweetie. I'm almost done cooking," Mommy replied. Just then, a man knocked on the door. He was selling fruits and vegetables. "Hello, would you like to buy some fresh fruits and vegetables?" the man asked. "Yes, please!" Mommy said. "Lily, do you want to help me pick out some yummy fruits and vegetables to eat?" Lily nodded and they went outside to buy some food. They were both very happy and excited to eat their healthy snacks.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved playing with his tools, especially his hammer. He would use it to build all sorts of things, like forts and toy cars. One day, Timmy's friend Billy came over to play. Billy wanted to play with Timmy's hammer, but Timmy didn't want to share it. "No, Billy. This is my tool. I don't want to give it to you," said Timmy. Billy was sad and went home. Later that day, Timmy's dad came home from work and saw Timmy playing with his hammer. "Wow, Timmy! You're doing a great job. I'm so proud of you for using your tool to build something amazing," said Timmy's dad. Timmy felt happy and proud. He realized that sharing his tool with Billy would have been a nice thing to do. The next day, when Billy came over to play, Timmy offered to share his hammer. Billy was happy and they played together, displaying their creations proudly.

Once upon a time there was a vine who loved to print things. No matter what anyone said he liked to print things all by himself. He was very independent.
