Sarah brought a great big piece of cake to share with Lily. Lily's eyes got big when she saw the cake. "That looks yummy!" she said. "Do you dare me to eat the whole piece?" Sarah laughed. "I don't think you can eat the whole thing, it's too big!" Lily grinned. "I can do it, just watch me!" So, Lily took a big bite of the cake. It was so delicious that she couldn't stop eating it. Soon, the whole piece was gone! "Wow, you did it!" Sarah exclaimed. "You're a great cake eater!" Lily smiled with pride. "Thanks, I love cake!"

Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Amy. She was always curious and wanted to explore the world. One day, while exploring her garden, she stumbled across a little puppy. The puppy was so attractive and eager to play, so Amy decided to take the puppy home and protect it. After a long time of playing and exploring together, Amy and the puppy had become best friends. Every day, they did all sorts of fun activities, like exploring the park and going on long walks. The two were so close that they even talked to each other. One day, while they were walking, the puppy stopped and looked up at Amy. "Why do you protect me all the time?" the puppy asked. Amy smiled and said, "Because I love you, and I want us to be friends for a long time."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the forest behind her house. One day, she saw a thin tree that looked different from all the others. Lily wanted to examine it closer, so she walked over to it. As she got closer, she saw that there were little bugs crawling on the tree. Lily thought they looked funny and she giggled. She examined the bugs and then looked at the tree again. She noticed that the bark was peeling off in some places. Lily decided to go back home and tell her mommy about the special tree she found. She ran back to her house and told her mommy all about the thin tree with the funny bugs. Her mommy smiled and said they would go back to the forest together to examine it more closely.

Once upon a time, there was a big farm with lots of animals. There was a bull named Billy who loved to eat grass all day long. One day, the farmer noticed that Billy was looking a little sad. He realized that Billy needed some new hay to eat. So, the farmer went to the barn to supply Billy with some fresh hay. Billy was so happy and started eating it right away. The hay was so fancy and smelled so good that Billy felt like a king. From that day on, Billy was the happiest bull on the farm. He loved the farmer for always supplying him with the best hay. And the farmer loved Billy for being such a happy and healthy bull. The end.

There was a little girl named Julie. She was only 3 years old. One day she wanted to play with her ball. She ran outside to the garden with it. She was so happy. She ran and ran, kicking her ball further and further away from her. Suddenly she felt a sharp pain in her ankle. She started to cry. She wanted to go back inside but the pain was too strong. She sat down and cried. Just then, a yellow bird came to sit on a tree next to her. The bird started to chirp and flew around her. This made Julie stop crying. She looked up at the bird and smiled. The bird flew away but Julie wasn't sad anymore. She slowly walked back inside, trying to complete her game of ball. When she got back, her mom hugged her and kissed her pain away. Everything was complete again.

Once upon a time, there was a big fair in a small town. Many kids came to play games and have fun. One of the games had a big prize that everyone wanted to win. A little boy named Tim really wanted to win the prize. He played the game many times, but he did not win. Tim started to feel sad. His mom told him, "Believe in yourself, and you can do it!" Tim tried one more time. He played the game and won the prize! He was so happy. He showed his mom the prize and said, "I believed in myself, and I won!" They both smiled and enjoyed the rest of the fair.

Once upon a time there was a boy named Clem. He was nearly three years old. He was very happy and loved playing with his family. One day, his mum asked Clem if he wanted to go somewhere secret. Clem was so excited! He could not wait to find out what it was. Mum said, "Come, follow me". She took Clem to the garden and showed him a diary. It was pink and beautiful. She said, "Clem, I want to invite you to keep a secret diary. You can write or draw in it". Clem giggled and said, "Yes, I really want to do that!". Mum smiled and said, "Then I invite you to keep this secret diary". Clem started putting his thoughts and pictures in the diary. He was so happy to be able to keep a beautiful and attractive diary all for himself. He knew it was a special invitation and he accepted it with joy.

Mommy was walking with Sam, her three year old son, one day when they stopped at a park. "This looks like a nice park," Mommy said. Sam nodded and looked around. Everything was ordinary. Trees, birds, a bench, a play area. "Can I go to the play area?" asked Sam, pointing to a tall slide. "Yes," said Mommy. "Be careful, ok?" Sam grinned and ran over to the slide. He climbed up the steps, careful not to trip. When he reached the top, Sam sat down eagerly. Then he began to slide down quickly. Mommy watched, smiling. She was so proud of her son for taking care of himself. Sam's eyes sparkled with delight. He went down the slide again and again, faster and faster. Altogether, Sam and Mommy had a fun time at the park. Sam was happy, and Mommy was glad he was taking such good care of himself.

Ricky loved to pick things up around him. He lived on a farm and his favourite thing to pick up was the brown bull. Ricky would pet the bull's thick and soft fur before hugging him around the neck. The bull never moved and didn't seem to mind Ricky's hugs or pats. One day when Ricky went outside to pick up the bull, he saw something different. The bull had a big patch of bright yellow paint on its side. Ricky jumped up and down and clapped his hands in excitement. He ran to pick it up and noticed that the bull was wearing a big colourful flower on its ear. The bull liked the attention and followed Ricky all around the farm. Ricky got a large brush and started to carefully pick off the paint. He had picked away the paint and there was his brown bull back to normal. Ricky felt happy and hugged the bull's neck tight. He ran off to find his parents to show them his favourite brown bull.

One day, a hunter was walking through the forest. He was looking for something to bring back home. Suddenly, he heard a noise in the bushes. He stopped to listen. It sounded like something was in trouble. The hunter saw a little bunny tangled in a bush. He carefully took the bunny out of the bush and held it in his hands. The bunny was embarrassed, but the hunter was very kind. He talked to the bunny and made it feel safe. The hunter brought the bunny back home with him. He hugged the bunny tight and got it some food. He made sure it was happy and comfortable. The bunny was so grateful that the hunter came along and saved it. After that, the hunter and the bunny were best friends. The hunter would often bring food for the bunny and take long walks together. They were never embarrassed when they were together. They were happy to be friends forever.

Once there was a frog. He was hopping around looking for something to eat. All he could see was some leaves and some bugs, but he was still hungry. Suddenly he smelled something delicious. "What is that smell? I'm so hungry!" croaked the frog. He hopped over to the smell and found a man cooking something on the fire. "What are you cooking?" asked the frog. "I'm cooking some yummy food. Would you like some?" said the man, who was very friendly. The frog was so excited. He said, "Yes please! Food would be available to me." The man gave the frog some of the food and the frog ate it hungrily. It was the best thing he had ever tasted! "Thank you so much," said the frog happily. "You were so kind to share your food with me. I'm so lucky!" The man smiled and said, "You're welcome. It's always nice to share what we have with others." From then on, the frog always remembered how kind the man had been and that there was always food available if you needed it.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Anna. She had a very kind aunt who always made her feel special and loved. Aunt Marie had promised Anna that she would come and visit her soon. One day, Anna's aunt finally arrived, and the house was tidy and ready for her. Anna's aunt had brought many special surprises for Anna. Anna was so excited, she could hardly contain her joy. Aunt Marie hugged Anna and promised her they would have lots of fun while she was visiting. To show her excitement, Anna gave her aunt a big hug and thanked her for coming. The days went by quickly as Anna's aunt taught her many things. They danced, sang, played games and even went for a walk. Finally, it was time for Aunt Marie to leave and Anna was feeling sad. Before leaving, Aunt Marie promised Anna that she would visit again soon. Anna smiled, feeling happy once again. She waved goodbye to her aunt as she drove away and looked forward to seeing her again.

Once upon a time, there was a bunny named Benny. Benny loved to hop around in the fields and eat wheat. One day, Benny got lost and couldn't find his way back home. He felt scared and sad. Suddenly, he saw a kind farmer who noticed Benny was lost. The farmer took Benny home and gave him some yummy carrots to eat. Benny was so grateful to the farmer for helping him. From that day on, Benny visited the farmer every day to say thank you and hop around in the fields. He loved his new friend and was happy to have someone who cared for him.

Mum and Dad were never home. Their 3 year old, Emma, was often bored and had nobody to talk to. One day, when Emma looked out of the window, she saw her neighbour outside, watching TV. Emma was envious of her neighbour, so she decided to do the same. She went to the living room, looking for the TV. She pressed many buttons, but the screen stayed blank. "Where is the TV?" cried Emma. Suddenly, her mum appeared in the doorway. "Emma, we don't have a TV," said Mum. Emma looked crestfallen, and her mum felt sorry for her. "Let's go to the park instead," said Mum. Emma smiled, and her mum put on her coat. Then they both went outside, to play in the sunshine.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly little dog named Rover. Rover loved to explore and have fun. One day, he decided to take a walk with his mom. Rover and his mom walked and walked until Rover's feet started to get tired. But Rover was so excited to keep walking, he didn't want to delay. His mom suggested taking a break, but Rover was keen to continue on exploring. So his mom agreed, as long as he took some breaks so his feet wouldn't get too tired. Rover and his mom kept on walking and eventually, Rover's feet didn't hurt so much anymore. Rover was jolly and he felt better after the break. The two kept on walking until they got home and Rover laughed and smiled the whole way. He was so happy he was able to keep up and not delay.

Once upon a time there was a Puppy who wanted to go into the Baby's room. He saw the door to the Baby's room and started barking loudly. The Baby's Mom told the Puppy to stop making noise, and asked the Puppy to behave. The Puppy was very curious and he wanted to go inside the room. He tried to open the door quickly but it wasn't budging. Mom told the Puppy to be patient and reached down to turn the door handle. The door opened slowly, and Mom asked the Puppy to be careful and to come inside very slowly. The Puppy followed Mom inside the Baby's room and saw the Baby sleeping under a blanket. Mom said, ''See, that's why we must always behave and not make too much noise. We don't want to wake the Baby up and scare her!". The Puppy understood and from then on, he tried to be very quiet around the Baby and to always open the door slowly. This story tells us that we must behave and be patient around people, and not act too quickly!

Once upon a time, there was a little ant who woke up early in the morning. The ant wanted to crawl out of its nest to get some food. But when the ant got outside, it saw a big spider who had eaten all the food. The ant felt sad and hungry. The spider saw the ant and felt guilty for eating all the food. The spider said sorry to the ant and promised to share the food. The ant and the spider became friends and shared the food every morning. They crawled together to find the best food and had fun adventures. One day, they found a big piece of cake. The ant and the spider were so happy and ate the cake together. They were no longer hungry and felt very happy. From that day on, they always shared their food and had fun together. The end.

Bob was walking down the street when he noticed his wife. She was carrying a bag and she looked very happy. Bob wanted to say hello, but he was feeling a bit foolish. So he just waved instead. His wife waved back, but then she turned and walked away. Bob felt sad and wondered why she seemed so distant. When he got home, he asked his wife what was wrong. She explained that she was going to the store to buy a party hat. It was their wedding anniversary and she wanted to surprise him with a party. Bob was so happy and felt silly for not realising. From then on, he never tried to be too clever and just enjoyed the moment. He was very glad he noticed his wife.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to jog around his backyard every day. One day, Timmy saw his neighbor, Billy, jogging too. Timmy said, "Hi Billy, do you want to jog with me?" Billy said, "Yes, I would love to jog with you, Timmy!" So they started jogging together. After a while, Timmy's mom came outside with a jug of fuel for the lawnmower. Billy looked at the jug and said, "I'm envious of your fuel, Timmy. My dad needs to buy some for our lawnmower." Timmy's mom overheard Billy and said, "Don't worry, Billy. We have plenty of fuel to share. Here, take some for your dad's lawnmower." Billy was so happy and thanked Timmy's mom. From that day on, Timmy and Billy jogged together every day and shared their fuel and toys with each other. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a girl who was open. She liked to talk to everyone and share her secrets. One day, she met a lemon. The lemon said it would be her friend. The girl trusted the lemon and said she could keep her secrets safe. The lemon kept the girl's secrets. But one day, when the girl shared a new secret, the lemon said it was too big and could not keep it. The girl was sad, so the lemon said it know someone else who could keep the secret safe. The girl trusted the lemon's friend. But it was a bad decision. The lemon's friend told everyone the secret. The girl felt so angry and sad. The girl learned that it is not always good to trust. She should open her eyes and be more careful when trusting others.

Once upon a time, in a far away land, lived a family of three. There was the father, the mother, and the little one. Every day, the family gathered around the table to prepare yummy meals. The little one loved watching the parents work. One day, the father said, "Let's go on a family trip! We will climb a big and dirty volcano!" The little one was so excited to go! So the family packed their things and started a long journey. After a few days, they finally arrived at the volcano. The little one looked up to the sky and was amazed by the height of the dirty volcano. It was time to prepare for the climb. The father and the mother strapped on their walking shoes and the little one grabbed a climbing stick. Slowly, the family started climbing the volcano. Eventually, before the top of the volcano, the family stopped to take a break and finally reached the peak of the dirty mountain. The little one was so happy because at the peak of the dirty volcano was a large, beautiful lake. The father, mother and the little one celebrated their successful climb together!

One day, a girl named Lily went to a store with her mom. They wanted to buy a toy for Lily's friend, who was having a birthday party. In the store, there were many toys. Some toys were big, some were small, some were expensive, and some were not. Lily saw a toy that seemed very nice. It was a big, red ball. But her mom said, "Lily, that ball is too expensive. We need to find a toy that has good value for your friend." So, they looked at more toys. After looking at many toys, Lily and her mom found a toy that was not expensive and had good value. It was a pretty doll with a yellow dress. Lily was happy, and her mom was happy too. They bought the doll for Lily's friend, and they all had a great time at the birthday party.

Once upon a time there were two ducks. One was called Blue and one was called Yellow. Yellow was so excited because Blue said she would provide him with a wing of his own. Blue said, â€œLetâ€™s go for a walk and see if we can find a smooth wing for you.â€ So, they started walking through the park and soon they spotted a big, white feather. Blue said, â€œThis looks like a perfect feather for you.â€ So, Yellow grabbed the feather and said â€œThank you Blue!â€ They both went back home with the feather and Blue helped Yellow fasten the smooth feather to his back. Yellow was so delighted and couldnâ€™t stop flapping his new wing. The moral of the story is that itâ€™s important to help your friends and provide them with what they need.

Once upon a time, there was a little birdie. The birdie wanted to fly high up in the sky. So, it flapped its wings and flew up, up, up! It saw a long line of other birdies flying in the same direction. The little birdie joined the line and flew with them. After a while, the birdies got hungry. They saw a tree with some tasty fruits. The little birdie and its new friends landed on the tree and ate the tasty fruits. They were so full and happy. Then, they flew away again, higher and higher, until they reached their home in the clouds. The little birdie was so happy to have made new friends and had a tasty snack.

Once upon a time there was a princess. She lived in an amazing palace. Every night she looked out of her window and wished she could explore the world outside. One day, something magical happened - a giant appeared, like out of a storybook. He had a big smile and said he wanted to take the princess out of the palace. She nodded with excitement! The giant took the princess on an adventure around the world - they explored snowy mountains, deep oceans, and dense forests. Everywhere they went was more amazing than the last. At the end of the day, the giant took the princess back to the palace. He said goodbye and thanked her for such a fun adventure. She smiled and nodded happily. Every night the princess looked at the stars and remembered her amazing adventure with the giant. She would never forget it!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in her garden. Her garden was very pretty and had lots of flowers. One day, Lily's mom told her that she would receive a surprise package in the mail. Lily was so excited! She waited and waited for the package to arrive. Finally, the mailman came and gave her the package. Lily opened it and found a new toy to play with in her clean garden. She was so happy! She played with her new toy in the garden all day long. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. He had two thumbs, one on each hand. But his left thumb was different from his right thumb. One day, Timmy and his friends played a game. They had to throw a ball into a basket. The first one to get three balls in the basket would win. Timmy tried and tried, but he couldn't get any balls in the basket. But then, on his last try, he threw the ball and it went straight into the basket! Timmy won the game and his friends cheered for him. He was so happy that he jumped up and down and showed his different thumbs to everyone.

Once there was a little girl named Mia. She liked to make a mess, but her mom did not like that very much! Every time Mia made a mess her mom would give her a lecture. Mia did not want to listen and would always disagree. One day Mia was being really messy. Her mom had a lecture for her longer than usual. Mia did not like the lecture and disagreed. She said she would find a better way to clean up her mess. Her mom said she was very brave and helped her clean up. When it was all clean, Mia's mom gave her a big hug. She said that rules are important, but that it is also ok to disagree. Mia smiled and hugged her mom back. She was so happy that she found a way to disagree and still get a hug!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play sport with his friends. They would run and jump and throw balls. One day, Timmy and his friends went to the park to play. They wanted to play soccer, but they did not have a ball. So, they looked around the park to gather things they could use. They found a big stick and some leaves, but they were not good for playing soccer. They were patient and kept looking until they found an old ball. They were so happy and played soccer all day long.

Once upon a time, there was a wild horse who lived in a big green field. He ran and played all day long. One day, a little girl came to the field and saw the horse. She was gifted with a carrot and wanted to give it to the horse. But the horse didn't want the carrot and sighed. The little girl felt sad and went away. The horse felt bad for sighing and wished he had taken the carrot. So, the next time the little girl came, the horse happily accepted the carrot and they became friends forever.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a loop in the park. He loved the loop so much. It was big and round. Tim wanted to show his friend, Sue, his new loop. Tim went to Sue's house and said, "Look at my loop! I love it so much!" But Sue did not like the loop. She thought it was harsh. "I don't like your loop, Tim. It's too harsh," Sue said. This made Tim very sad. He wanted Sue to love the loop too. Tim thought about what he could do. He had an idea. He put a soft cover on the loop. Now the loop was not harsh anymore. Tim showed Sue the new loop. Sue said, "Now I love the loop too, Tim! It's not harsh anymore." Tim and Sue played with the loop all day. They were both very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big brown bear. The bear loved to eat honey from the beehive. One day, the bear met a little girl. The girl had a label on her shirt that said her name was Lily. "Hello, Lily," said the bear. "What are you doing?" "I'm looking for my toy," said Lily. "I lost it in the woods." The bear thought for a moment. "I'll help you find it," he said, nodding his head. Lily was very grateful. "Thank you, bear," she said. "You're very thoughtful." Together, they searched the woods until they found the toy. Lily hugged the bear and they became good friends. From then on, the bear would often visit Lily and they would play together.

Once upon a time, there was a big, big bear. The big bear loved to sleep in the forest. He would sleep all day long. The big bear was very happy when he slept. One day, while the big bear was sleeping, a little fox came into the forest. The little fox saw the big bear sleeping and thought it was funny. The little fox started to play tricks on the big bear. The big bear did not like this. The big bear felt a threat from the little fox. The big bear woke up and saw the little fox. The big bear roared and scared the little fox away. The little fox ran away and never came back. The big bear went back to sleep, happy and safe. And from that day on, the big bear could sleep without any threats.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly man who loved to paint. He wanted to create a special landscape. Every day he got up early and painted the sky, with its clouds and stars. He painted the trees, the flowers and the animals. He painted mountains and rivers. He painted the sun and the moon and all the other things that make up a landscape. As he was painting, people came and watched him. Everyone said he was very good at painting. They said it was like he was creating a magical place. He worked for days and days and eventually his painting was complete. Everyone who saw it was amazed at how beautiful it was. Even though it was just a painting, it was like a real landscape. The friendly man was very happy that his painting was complete. He smiled as he looked at it - he had made a very special picture.

Mum and her two children were in the bathroom. She put the little brother in the bath, and the older sister watched as he floated in the water. She smiled at him, and he smiled back. Mum lathered the soap and washed the brother with it. He laughed as the bubbles foamed up on his skin. He was having so much fun! After he was clean, Mum turned on the shower and the brother started to giggle. The water was cold, but it felt so good on his skin. Mum put some sweet-smelling shampoo on the brother's hair, and he loved it. She smoothed it through his hair until it was all shiny and clean. The brother was so happy when they were finished. He looked at his sister and smiled. She smiled back, and they went back to their room.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to walk with his cane. He would take it with him everywhere he went. One day, Tim went for a walk in the park. He walked and walked, but he couldn't find his cane. He looked everywhere, but it was nowhere to be found. Tim was very sad. He sat down on a bench and started to cry. Suddenly, he heard a noise behind him. He turned around and saw his persistent little dog, Max, carrying his cane in his mouth. Tim was so surprised and happy to see his cane again. He hugged Max and gave him a big kiss. From that day on, Tim and Max went on many walks together with Tim's trusty cane.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to explore the world around him. One day, he found a noisy bug in his backyard. He wanted to see it up close, so he asked his mom for her microscope. His mom said, "Sure, Timmy. But be careful with it." Timmy carefully looked at the bug through the microscope. He was amazed by how big the bug looked up close. Suddenly, he heard a loud noise. It was his little sister playing with her toys. Timmy asked his sister, "Can you please be quiet? I'm trying to look at this bug." His sister replied, "Okay, Timmy. I'll be quiet." Timmy continued to look at the bug through the microscope. He even got down on his knees to get a better look. He was so happy he found the bug and was able to see it up close.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to swing at the park. One day, she saw a big statue of a lion. "That statue is so big!" she said to her mom. "Yes, it is," her mom replied. Suddenly, Lily saw a small bird flying towards her. She got frightened and wanted to get off the swing. "Mommy, there's a bird!" she exclaimed. "It's okay, the bird won't hurt you," her mom said. Lily watched as the bird landed on the statue's head. "Look, Mommy! The bird is on the lion's head!" she said. "Yes, it is," her mom replied with a smile. Lily continued to swing and watch the bird on the statue. She wasn't frightened anymore and enjoyed her time at the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sally. She lived in a small house with her mommy and daddy. Sally had a neighbor named Mr. Brown. Mr. Brown was a nice man, but he had an ugly hat. The hat was big and green with a red bow. Sally did not like the hat, but she liked Mr. Brown. One day, Sally saw the ugly hat on the ground outside Mr. Brown's house. She wanted to help, so she picked up the hat. Sally walked to Mr. Brown's door and knocked. When Mr. Brown opened the door, Sally put the ugly hat in his hand. She said, "I found your hat, Mr. Brown." Mr. Brown smiled and thanked Sally. He put the ugly hat on his head and said, "Now my head will not be cold." Sally was happy that she helped her neighbor. From that day on, she thought the ugly hat was not so bad after all.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, Jenny and Kevin. They went on an adventure to a far away place. As they walked, Jenny noticed a jar by the side of the road. She stopped to pick it up and asked her friend, "What could be inside the jar?" Kevin replied, "Maybe some yummy food!" Jenny was very excited and opened the jar. To her surprise, the jar was full of ants! She was a bit disappointed, but she also noticed how hard the ants were working. Suddenly, Jenny felt very hungry. She asked Kevin, "Do you have something to eat? Iâ€™m so hungry!" Kevin had an idea. He took some food from his pocket, and the food he had was enough to fill the jar. The two friends shared the food and the ants had more than enough for the whole day. Jenny and Kevin learnt an important lesson that day. Even when we are faced with hunger and need something to eat, we should think of others before ourselves. Helping out becomes much easier when we put the needs of others before our own.

Once upon a time, there were two good friends, Jack and John. They went out to play one day, but then a problem arose. Jack found a trunk with a lot of money in it. He wanted to keep it all for himself. John said, "Please don't. It's not yours." But Jack was being very foolish and he wouldn't listen. So John started to cry. The tears made Jack feel bad, so he changed his mind. He shared the money with John, so they could both be happy. The moral of this story is that it's better to be kind and share with others than to do something foolish.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in the park near her house. One day, she saw a big river in front of her. She wanted to cross it but there was no bridge. Lily went to her dad and asked him to provide a bridge for her to cross the river. Her dad said he would try his best. But the next day, when they went to the park, there was still no bridge. Lily was sad and decided to build a bridge by herself. She found some wood and started to put it together. It was a different kind of bridge, but it worked. She crossed the river and was very proud of herself. But when she came back, she saw that the river had flooded and her bridge was gone. Lily was scared and sad because she couldn't cross the river anymore. She learned that sometimes, even if we try our best, things don't always work out the way we want them to.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl. She wanted to go on an adventure. So, one day she packed her bag and said goodbye to her family. She set off and walked down a long path. After quite a while, she arrived at a river. To her surprise, there was a boat there! She got in and sailed away. The boat sailed for a long time before it arrived at a new land. The little girl got out and looked around. It was very quiet and very tidy. Then, she heard a sound. She followed the sound until she arrived at a big house. Inside the house, she found a family of moles. They were eating lunch together. The family were very friendly and invited her to join them. They laughed and talked until the sun went down. Finally, the little girl said goodbye and thanked the moles for the wonderful day. She waved as she sailed away in her boat. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day, a little girl named Jane went to the park. She saw a small, humble dog with a toy. The toy was a fun object. Jane wanted to play with the dog and the toy. Jane went to the dog and asked, "Can I play with you and your toy?" The dog wagged its tail and barked. Jane knew the dog said yes. They played together all day. Jane threw the toy and the dog ran to get it. They had a lot of fun. In the end, Jane and the humble dog became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved playing with his toy cars and trucks all day. But one day, he saw his daddy putting on a big, yellow helmet. "Where are you going, Daddy?" Timmy asked. "I have to go to work, son," Daddy said. Timmy didn't like it when his daddy had to go to work. He felt lonely without him. But Daddy promised to come back soon and give Timmy a big hug. Later that day, Timmy heard a loud noise outside. He ran to the window and saw his daddy's big, yellow truck coming down the street. Daddy had come back from work, just like he promised! Timmy was so happy, he ran outside and gave his daddy a big hug.

The toddler went outside. He was feeling calm and saw a bright white bird. He smiled and nodded to it. But suddenly, the sky got dark. The toddler felt scared and the white bird flew away. Rain started pouring down and the wind started to blow. The toddler didnâ€™t know what to do. But then he saw a big tree and he ran to it. The big tree kept him calm and safe from the wind. He nodded at the tree in thanks. He waited for the storm to pass and when it finally did, the sun was shining again. The white bird returned and the toddler smiled and nodded. The bird flew up and the toddler went back home. He was happy and calm.

One night, Tim was feeling tired. He was ready to go to sleep but he heard a sound. It was a strange sound. So, Tim got his flashlight and turned it on to look around. He could see something furry coming towards him. It was a kitten! Tim was so excited and wanted to spoil the little kitten. He gave it lots of cuddles and scratches and played with it for a while. When the kitten was full and tired from playing, Tim made a comfortable bed for it and tucked it in. Then he said goodnight, turned off his flashlight, and went to bed. It was the best sleepy time ever!

Once there was a pupil who was very creative. He liked to use his imagination to come up with different ideas. One day, he had a great idea. He decided he was going to try and win a special competition - a race! He practised and practised with all his might, so that he could become faster. On the day of the competition, he was ready to win! He made sure that he had his running shoes and a bottle of water. The race started, and the pupil sprinted forward. He ran as fast as he could, and finally, he crossed the finish line first. He had won the race! Everyone cheered and congratulated the pupil on his victory. He was so proud of himself. He knew that all his hard work, practice and creativity had paid off. He had won the race and achieved something special, and it made him very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a bunny named Benny. Benny loved to jump and play in the grass. One day, Benny saw a race happening and wanted to join. "Can I race too?" Benny asked the other animals. "Sure, but you have to win to get a prize," said the raccoon. Benny was excited and started running as fast as he could. But Benny was not the fastest. He tried his best, but he could not win. "I wanted to win," Benny said sadly. "It's okay, Benny," said the kind deer. "You tried your best." Benny was still sad, but then he saw a shiny trophy. "I want that trophy," Benny said. "I'll buy it for you," said the sneaky fox. "It's cheap, I promise." Benny was happy and gave the fox all his money. But the fox was lying. The trophy was not cheap, and it was not even real. Benny had been tricked. He was sad and had no money. "I wish I didn't buy that trophy," Benny said. Benny learned a lesson that day. He learned to be careful and not trust everyone.

Once upon a time, there was a fierce lion cub who wanted to play with the other animals in the jungle but he didnâ€™t know how. The lion cub decided to ask the other animals for help. â€œCan you please teach me how to play?â€ the lion cub asked the squirrel. The squirrel thought for a moment and said, â€œI can share a way to play with the other animals. Itâ€™s called being nice.â€ So, the lion cub tried being kind and friendly with the other animals. He shared his food and helped them when they were in trouble. After some time, the lion cub was no longer fierce. He was kind and gentle, and the other animals soon accepted him as one of their own. The moral of the story is that being kind and sharing can help you make new friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a favorite teddy bear named Mr. Buttons. Mr. Buttons had been with Lily since she was a baby, and he was very loyal to her. One day, Lily and Mr. Buttons were playing outside when they saw a butterfly. Lily wanted to catch it, but Mr. Buttons said, "No, Lily. We must remain still and watch the butterfly fly." Lily was disappointed that she couldn't catch the butterfly, but she listened to Mr. Buttons and they watched the butterfly together. After a while, the butterfly flew away and Lily turned to Mr. Buttons and said, "Thank you for teaching me to be patient, Mr. Buttons. You are the best friend ever." Mr. Buttons smiled and said, "I will always be loyal to you, Lily." And they hugged each other tightly.

Once there was an ancient pot. It sat alone in an empty room. One day, a little girl came in with a bag of rice. She slowly walked over to the pot and opened the bag. She gently poured the rice into the pot until it was full. Then she noticed something special. On the side of the pot there was a button. She pressed it and suddenly a beautiful light filled the room. The pot began to glow and the rice started to dance. The little girl was so excited that she started to dance too! They all danced joyfully until there was no more light in the room. The ancient pot was content and the little girl was delighted. They continued to pour rice into the pot whenever they wanted to have a dance party.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily was playing in the park. Suddenly, she saw a thief with a white shirt run away with her toy. Lily shouted, "Stop thief! Give me my toy back!" The thief kept running, but Lily was fast too. She ran after the thief and caught him by his shirt. "Give me my toy back, please," Lily said. The thief was surprised and scared. He gave Lily the toy back and ran away as fast as he could. Lily was happy to have her toy back and ran home to show it to her mom. Her mom was proud of her bravery and gave her a big hug.

Once upon a time, Jack was playing in the park. Suddenly â€“ something mysterious caught Jack's eye. It was a mask! Jack thought it looked cool and picked it up. He put it on and it fit perfectly. Jack really liked it and he decided to take it home. When he got home, he showed his mom the mysterious mask. His mom said he could keep it, as long as he took good care of it. Jack put the mask on every single day. He loved playing with it. One day, while playing outside, he left the mask in the grass. He started to look for it, but it had disappeared! He was so worried and started crying. But then, Jack remembered something his mom had said. She said that when he loses something, he should always look for it later in the same place - and just like that, the mask reappeared! Jack was so happy that he hugged it tight. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Jack. He was just three years old. Jack wanted something special for his birthday. So his mommy surprised him with a giant chocolate cake! Jack was so excited to see the cake. He asked his mommy, â€œCan I have a slice?â€ His mommy said, â€œYes, but make sure you value every bite. It's a very special treat.â€ Jack nodded and began to eat the cake. He was so happy and it tasted really yummy. He ate a big slice and said, â€œMmmmm. It's the best cake in the world!â€ Then Jack's big sister came in the room. She saw the cake and said, â€œWow! Can I have a slice too?â€ Jack said, â€œYes, but make sure you value every bite. It's a very special treat.â€ Jack's mommy smiled as she watched them both enjoy the cake. She was so glad that they both valued the treat. Later that night, Jack and his sister took their loud laughter outside to the backyard. They ate the cake while looking up at the stars. They both valued every moment.

Once upon a time, there was a cute little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with clay. He would make fun shapes and animals with it. One day, Tim wanted to make a big clay house. He worked very hard to make it. But his little sister, Sue, wanted to play too. Sue tried to help Tim with the clay house. But she was too little and didn't know how to do it right. She made a big mess with the clay. Tim got mad at Sue. He didn't want her to play with his clay. Sue started to cry. Tim saw Sue cry and felt bad. He wanted to make her smile again. So, he decided to let Sue play with the clay. Together, they made a new clay house. It was not perfect, but it was fun. Tim and Sue smiled and played with the clay house all day long. And they became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy was very stubborn. He never wanted to do what his mommy and daddy told him to do. One day, Timmy got very sick and his mommy took him to see the doctor. The doctor was very nice and gave Timmy some medicine to make him feel better. After a few days, Timmy started to feel better and his mommy told him it was time to settle down and rest. Timmy listened to his mommy and went to bed early. The next day, Timmy felt much better and he knew it was because he listened to his mommy and the doctor.

One day, Tom was walking around the beach. He was looking for something special, but he didn't know what it was. Then he found the reef. It was big and full of colourful fish. Tom thought it was the coolest thing he had ever seen. After a while, a stupid fish jumped out. It swam close to Tom, as if it wanted to be friends. Tom was so excited to find a new friend. He gave the stupid fish a name - Steve. Tom and Steve spent the day swimming and exploring the reef. Tom laughed at Steve's silly tricks. They even found some cool shells to collect. By the end of the day, Tom was very tired. He was sad to say goodbye to Steve, but they promised they would meet again soon. Tom and Steve had found a wonderful day of friendship at the reef.

Once there was a boy called Tom. Tom's mummy brought him a thermometer from the shop. It was an original thermometer, not a copy. Tom was very excited to see it. He wanted to show it to all of his friends, so he brought the thermometer to school. At school, all of Tom's friends were very impressed! They loved the original thermometer and wanted to touch it. Tom was very proud to show it off. He brought it back home to keep it safe. Now, Tom and his mummy can use the thermometer to measure everyone's temperature when they are not feeling well.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily's mommy took her to the store to buy some candy. Lily was very happy and excited. She picked out her favorite candy and ate it all up. But then, Lily started to feel very sleepy. When they got home, Lily went to take a nap. But she had trouble falling asleep because her tummy hurt from eating too much candy. Her mommy told her that eating too much candy can spoil your tummy and make you feel sick. From that day on, Lily learned that eating too much candy can cause trouble and make you feel sleepy. She promised to only eat a little bit of candy at a time so that she wouldn't get sick again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to collect shiny things like rocks and beads. One day, she went to the store with her mom and saw a pretty gem in the toy section. It was very shiny and sparkly, but it cost a lot of money. Lily's mom said it was too expensive, but Lily really wanted it. She asked her mom if there was a cheaper one, and her mom said they could look. They found another gem that was not as shiny, but it was a good number cheaper. Lily was very happy and took the gem home. She added it to her collection and showed it to all her friends. They thought it was very pretty too. From that day on, Lily knew that sometimes, things can be just as good even if they are not the most expensive.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, Max and Jane. Max was strong, while Jane was not. Max decided to show her how strong he was, and so he said he would lift a heavy bucket. Jane thought Max was silly, and so she laughed. Max picked up the heavy bucket and carried it over to Jane. He said, "See! I'm strong enough to lift this bucket!" Jane clapped for him. She looked admiringly and said, "I wish I was as strong as you Max". Max smiled and said, "You don't have to be strong like me. You just need to work together with your friends â€“ it's easy then!" Jane was surprised by this. She said, "That's true, Max! Working together is much easier!" They both laughed, gave each other a big hug, and went off be happily ever after. The moral of the story is that if you work together with others, you can do big and heavy things much more easily.

Once upon a time there was a girl. She was so careful! One day, the girl went out jogging. She was being so careful not to step in anything bad. As she was jogging, she noticed something strange. It smelled like poison! The girl stopped jogging and stayed very still. She was so careful not to touch it. The girl saw a frog near the poison. The frog looked very scared. The girl wanted to help the frog. Very carefully, she picked the frog up and carried it away from the poison. The girl was so brave and strong. Once the girl had taken the frog away from the poison, she jogged off with it quickly. The frog was so happy and thanked the girl. The girl jogged all the way home. She was so proud that she was able to help the frog!

One day, Jack and his mom went to the park. Jack was very excited and started running around looking for things that he could play with and explore. Suddenly, he noticed a feather floating in the wind. Jack ran to catch the beautiful feather and was so excited. Jack ran back to his mom, holding the feather in his hand, but impatiently shifting from one foot to another. He asked his mom, "Can I keep it?" His mom smiled and replied, "Yes, of course you can keep it. Now, can you do me a favour? Can you please put it on the swing and see if it flutters and flies away?" Jack nodded and quickly ran off to put the feather on the swing. He watched the feather sway in the wind and it eventually flew away. Jack was so happy that he had let his feather go. He ran back to his mom with a big smile on his face and gave her a big hug. This was the perfect way to end Jack's day. He was happy and content as he and his mom left the park.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny. The bunny was very curious and loved to explore. One day, the bunny hopped into a big forest. The sun was shining, but the bunny felt hot. So, the bunny looked for a shade to rest. As the bunny rested, it saw a little bird weeping. The bunny felt sad and asked the bird why it was weeping. The bird said it lost its nest and didn't know where to go. The bunny felt bad for the bird and decided to help. The bunny hopped around the forest, looking for a new home for the bird. Finally, the bunny found a cozy tree with a big branch. The bunny helped the bird build a new nest on the branch. The bird was happy and thanked the bunny. The bunny felt happy too, knowing it helped a friend in need.

Once upon a time, there was a thoughtful king. He was very nice and kind. The king liked to help all the people in his land. One day, the king went for a walk. He saw a little girl crying. She had lost her toy. The king wanted to help her. He said, "Don't cry, little girl. I will take you to find your toy." The king took the girl by the hand. They walked and looked everywhere. At last, they found the toy under a big tree. The girl was very happy. She said, "Thank you, king!" The people in the land saw how the king helped the girl. They learned to be thoughtful too. They helped each other and were happy. The moral of the story is to be kind and help others.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Emma. She was always excited to go out with her mom to catch yummy treats. One day, Emma asked her mom, "Where are we going?" Her mom said, "We are going out to catch something special today. I'm sure you'll like it." Emma said, "What is it? Can I help?" Her mom said, "Yes, we're going to catch a pie!" Emma liked the sound of that and was excited to go. When they got to the shop, Emma saw a big, round pie sitting on the counter. She wanted to catch it, but it was too big. So they asked the shopkeeper if they could have a little pie. The shopkeeper served them a little pie and Emma caught it with her little hands. She was so delighted that she jumped for joy! The little girl and her mom were so happy about catching the pie!

Once upon a time, there was a happy fish named Nemo. Nemo swam in the bright blue sea every day with his friends. One day, they found a big reef with lots of colorful coral. Nemo wanted to explore the reef, but it was too deep for him to swim down. So, he asked his friend Dory to help him. Dory was happy to help and she swam down to the reef. Nemo watched as Dory kneel down and looked closely at the coral. She found a hidden treasure and brought it back to Nemo. They were both very excited and swam back to their friends to show them the treasure. From that day on, Nemo and Dory visited the reef often, and they always found new and exciting things to discover. They never forgot the bright colors of the coral and the fun they had exploring together.

Once upon a time, there was a boy. He was inspired by the stories of successful people. He wanted to be successful, too. One day, the boy went outside to play. He picked up a stick and a rock and started to match them. Soon, he was matching the stick and rock together in a variety of shapes and sizes. The boy was so pleased with himself. He said to himself, â€œI can do this! I can be successful.â€ He was excited and felt more confident. The boy went inside and continued to match the stick and rock together. His mom saw what he was doing and asked him, â€œWhat are you doing?â€ The boy responded, â€œMom, I am matching these together. I want to be successful.â€ His mom smiled and said, â€œYour hard work will make you successful, my son.â€ The boy was encouraged and kept matching the stick and rock together. He worked hard and kept believing in himself. He was eventually successful.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play outside in the sun with his friends. One day, Timmy found a powerful flashlight in his dad's garage. He thought it was so cool and decided to play with it. Timmy shined the flashlight on the ground and saw his shadow. He giggled and jumped around with his shadow. But then, he accidentally dropped the flashlight and it broke. Timmy was sad and knew he had to return it to his dad. When Timmy returned home, he was scared to tell his dad about the broken flashlight. But when he did, his dad was very angry and punished Timmy. Timmy felt so bad and wished he never played with the flashlight.

Once upon a time, there was a big tent in a park. Many kids came to see what was inside. They were all very happy. In the tent, there was a man who could perform rare tricks. He could make a ball float in the air. He could also make a rabbit come out of a hat. The kids clapped and laughed. The man and the kids had a lot of fun in the tent. The man performed more tricks, and the kids loved it. They all went home with big smiles on their faces.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Sophie. Sophie liked to run. Every day she would go for a big run in her village. One day, she went for a run and came upon a big building. It was a library. Sophie had never seen a library before and she was so glad. She ran right in. Inside the library, Sophie found lots of interesting things. She found books and games and puzzles. She was so happy! "Oh!" Sophie said. "What a fun place!" Suddenly, an old man in the library smiled at her and said "Would you like to borrow some books today, Sophie?â€ Sophie was so grateful. She took three books home. She was so happy and couldn't wait to read them. The next day she went for a run, but this time, instead of going to the library, she went to the park and played with her friends. She was so glad she had found the library and was grateful she had something new to do.

Once there was a girl. Her name was Joan. Joan loved to play. Every day she would go to the park, and laugh and play with her friends. One day it was very hot outside, but Joan still wanted to play. Her mother said, â€œJoan, you must take a nap and prevent getting too hot.â€ So Joan lay down and tried to go to sleep. But she was too excited and couldnâ€™t have a wake! Then she had an idea. She went out to the park again, and played in the shade. She stayed there until it was time to come home. She felt tired and relaxed, and had been successful in preventing getting too hot. Joan went to bed, happy and content. She had learnt that she could prevent getting too hot by playing in the shade.

Once, there was a very important bell. Everyday, the bell would ring loudly. Everyone in the village heard the bell. It was a sign that something special was happening. One day, the bell rang and everyone gathered around the village square. The mayor of the village said that today was a special day. He included everyone in a special game. Everyone was so excited and happy. The mayor said to stay very quiet and to all close their eyes when the bell rang again. Everyone followed the mayorâ€™s instructions and the bell rang again. When the bell stopped ringing, everyone opened their eyes and the mayor held up a big box full of surprises! Everyone who was included was so happy. They all laughed and cheered for the surprises. From then on, the bell was very important to the village. It was always a sign that something special was happening and that everyone was included in it.

Once upon a time there were two friends. They were called Bob and Ben. Bob loved music. One day, Ben asked Bob, "What do you like to do with music?" Bob smiled and said, "I like to bite it." Ben was so excited he said, "Bite it? Really?" Bob nodded. "Yes, it feels very comfortable in my mouth!" he said. Ben laughed and said, "That sounds silly!" Bob just smiled and said, "Biting music is fun!"

Once upon a time there was a brave girl who was eager to try new things. She wanted to join the club at her school. Every day after school, she would watch the other kids in the club, and it made her even more eager to be part of it. One day, she finally got the courage to ask if she could join the club. But the club said no, even though she tried her best to be brave. The girl was so sad. But the girl kept trying. She kept watching the other kids from a distance and she kept asking the club every day. Finally, after much struggle, the club decided to let her join. The girl was so happy! She had worked really hard and showed a lot of courage. Now she could be part of the club. She was going to have so much fun!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sunny. Sunny was very obedient and always did what her mommy and daddy said. One day, her mommy and daddy told her they were going on a special trip. Sunny was very excited and hoped it would be a lot of fun. When they arrived, they saw a big gun. Sunny was scared at first, but her mommy told her it was okay. She said the gun was only for protection. Sunny believed her and wasn't scared anymore. On the way home, Sunny told her mommy and daddy that she was thankful for the gun. She said it made her feel safe. Her mommy and daddy smiled and gave her a big hug. They were proud that Sunny was so obedient, and that she could believe in them. From then on, Sunny felt very safe whenever they went on trips. It was a happy ending!

Once there was a cat that liked to skip. He skipped around the house, everywhere he went. The cat was very happy and he enjoyed his skipping. One day he met a dog and the dog said, "Why do you skip?" The cat said, "I skip because it brings me joy." The dog was very ignorant and he said, "Why do you do something so silly?" The cat wasn't mad, instead he said, "Skipping brings me joy and it's fun. I don't understand why you don't join me." The dog didn't understand the joy that skipping could bring, so he just watched the cat skipping around and around. But eventually, he gave in and joined in. They both had a lot of fun skipping around the house, and everyone was happy.

Nina was so excited to go shopping with her mom. Her mom said she could have a new toy if she was good. As she was walking through the store, she saw a really nice toy, but it was too expensive for her pocket. Her mom said it was too expensive so they had to keep looking. But then Nina spotted an even better toy - it was cheap! She was so excited she grabbed it and put it in her pocket. Her mom didn't see, but when they got home and her mom noticed, she was very mad. She said it was wrong to take something without asking. Nina knew she was in trouble, and her mom had to punish her. She was very sad, but knew she had been wrong. From now on, she promised to be a good girl and always ask before she took anything.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a poppy in her garden. The poppy was red and pretty. But one day, Lily saw that the poppy looked dull. So, she took a cloth and polish the poppy. Now, the poppy was shining and looked beautiful again. Lily was happy and danced around the garden. The end.

Sandy was getting ready to go outside. He grabbed his watch and put it on his wrist. He picked out an ordinary blue shirt from his closet and put it on. On top of the shirt, he wore a warm coat. Finally, he put on his shoes and was ready to go. Sandy went outside and waited for his mom. He watched the people passing by. Some were walking, some were running and some were sitting on benches. Sandy felt excited to explore all the different things outside. Sandy's mom soon arrived and they went to the park. Sandy enjoyed the playground and running around the park. He saw some ducks in the water and began to chase them. He laughed and ran around until he became tired. Back at home, Sandy got undressed, hoping to wear his special watch again the next day. His mom tucked him in and he went to sleep, dreaming of all the fun things he got to do outside.

One day Mommy and Billy went to the store. Everything was so noisy! Daddy said they were going to buy something special - a print! Billy was excited. When they got to the store, Daddy told Billy to help fill it. Billy grabbed the big empty print and filled it with lots of colors. He was so excited to finish filling it. When the print was full, Daddy showed it to Billy. He said it was the perfect thing to hang up in the house. Mommy and Daddy smiled because it looked great! They all went home together, and Daddy hung the print up on the wall. Now it was all colorful and made the house look nice. Billy was so proud that he helped fill the print!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat candy and cakes all day long. Her mommy always told her that she needed to eat healthy food to grow strong and big. But Lily didn't listen. One day, Lily got an invitation to a fancy party. She was so excited to go and wear her pretty dress. But when she looked in the mirror, she saw that her dress was dirty and her shoes were scuffed. She didn't want to go to the party looking messy. So, Lily decided to polish her shoes and clean her dress. It took a lot of hard work, but she did it! When she arrived at the party, everyone said how beautiful she looked. After the party, Lily realized that just like polishing her shoes and cleaning her dress, she needed to polish her health by eating healthy food. From that day on, she started to eat fruits and vegetables and she felt strong and happy. The moral of the story is that it's important to take care of your health just like you take care of your appearance.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to collect shiny rocks and pretty flowers. One day, she went for a walk in the park with her mom. Lily was very busy looking for new things to collect. She searched under bushes and on top of rocks. Suddenly, she saw a butterfly flying by. "Look, Mommy! A butterfly!" she exclaimed. Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, it's very pretty. But don't forget, we are here to collect leaves for your art project." Lily nodded and continued to search for leaves. After a while, Lily's bag was full of leaves and she was very happy. "I collected so many leaves!" she said proudly. Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, you did a great job. Let's go home and make some art together." Lily smiled and held her bag of leaves tightly as they walked home.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his football. One day, he went outside to play with his football in the park. He felt a little troubled because he didn't have any friends to play with. While Tim was playing, he saw a small bird that looked troubled too. The bird had a hurt wing and could not fly. Tim wanted to help the bird, so he lay the football down next to the bird. He thought maybe the bird would feel better if they played together. Tim gently rolled the football to the bird. The bird looked at the football and then back at Tim. They started to play together and the bird felt better. Tim was happy because he made a new friend. From that day on, Tim and the bird played with the football every day and they were never troubled again.

Once there was a little girl named Lucy. Everywhere she went, people said 'welcome' to her. Today, Lucy was walking down the street. As she was walking, she saw so many new things. Then, Lucy noticed a green flower standing in the middle of the street. She went to it and said hello. Puzzled, the flower replied, â€œHello! Welcome to my home. What can I do for you?â€ Lucy was amazed! She was so excited to talk to a flower. She asked, â€œCan you tell me why you are green?â€ The flower replied, â€œWell, I think it is because I drink lots of water and get lots of sunshine. That helps me to stay healthy and green!â€ Lucy smiled. She thanked the flower for the information and continued walking down the street. Everywhere she went people said 'welcome' to her. Lucy had a wonderful day!

Tom and Lily are friends. They like to play in the park. Today is a hot day. They want to race to the big tree. "Ready, set, go!" Tom says. He runs fast. Lily runs fast too. They laugh and have fun. But then, Tom sees a rock. He does not see it in time. He trips on the rock. He falls down. He hurts his knee. He cries. Lily stops running. She sees Tom on the ground. She runs to him. She says, "Are you okay, Tom?" Tom says, "No, I am not okay. My knee is red and sore. It stings." Lily says, "I am sorry, Tom. Let me help you. We can go to the bench. We can get some water. We can put a band-aid on your knee." Tom says, "Thank you, Lily. You are a good friend." Lily helps Tom get up. They walk to the bench. They get some water. They put a band-aid on Tom's knee. They hug. They say, "We are still friends. We can race another day."

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a shy girl named Lily. She had a favorite glove that she would always wear. One day, she lost her glove while playing outside. Lily looked everywhere for her glove, but she couldn't find it. She saw her friend, Tim, and asked him, "Tim, have you seen my glove?" Tim said, "No, but I will help you look for it." They looked and looked, but they still couldn't find it. Then, they saw a big dog with something in its mouth. Lily said, "Look, the dog has my glove!" They went to the dog and asked, "Dog, can we have the glove back?" The dog refused to give it back. But when they gave the dog a treat, it finally let go of the glove. Lily was so happy to have her glove back, and she thanked Tim for helping her.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat sausages for breakfast. One day, her mom cooked her a sausage for breakfast, but it tasted sour. Lily didn't like it at all. She asked her mom to make her another one. Her mom made her another sausage, but this time Lily stepped on it by accident. She was sad because she couldn't eat it anymore. Her mom felt bad for her, so she made her a fresh sausage that wasn't sour. Lily was happy and ate it all up. From that day on, Lily was careful not to step on her sausages and she always made sure they weren't sour before she ate them. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a very selfish boy who loved to scare his friends. One day, he brought a gas mask to school and he wanted to scare everyone. He even put on the gas mask himself and then tried to scare his friends. His friends were scared and became angry. One of them said, "Why are you so selfish?" The boy realized that it wasn't nice to scare his friends. He told them, "I'm sorry. I won't do this again." His friends were happy and then all of them played together in the playground. They all had a lot of fun and the boy now knows better than to scare his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and run around with her friends. One day, her mom told her that she needed to study for a test at school. Lily didn't want to study, she wanted to play! But when Lily went to school the next day, she found out that the test was about weight. She didn't know anything about weight! The test was really tough and Lily didn't do well. She was sad and didn't want to go to school anymore. But then, Lily's teacher told her that it was okay to not know everything and that she could learn more about weight. Lily decided to study really hard and asked her teacher for help. She learned about weight and felt much better. The next time there was a test, Lily did really well and was proud of herself.

John was a brave boy. Every morning he hopped out of bed and went to school. His teacher gave him a folder every day and asked him to perform a new task. Yesterday, John had to count to ten. He was very brave and calmly looked at the folder. He saw the number one, so he began counting. He sang each number out loud and when he got to ten he felt very proud. This morning, his teacher handed him a folder. She told him he was very brave and could now perform different tasks. He opened the folder and saw a picture of a rocket. He was so excited! His teacher told him to draw a rocket on the paper. John was brave and drew the rocket. He used bright colors and was surprised with how good it looked. After he was finished, he held it up for everyone to see. Everyone clapped and praised him for being so brave. John was very proud of himself.

One day, there was a kind man who wanted to design something special. He thought about what he could make until he saw the sunrise. He thought it was so beautiful, so he decided to design something that he could see every sunrise. He worked hard and created a big machine. He showed it to his friend and said, â€œLook at my design, itâ€™s almost sunrise and I can see it from here!â€ His friend said, â€œThatâ€™s amazing! Can I see it?â€ The kind man said, â€œYes, you can see it!â€ They watched the sunrise together and it was a wonderful sight. Then the kind man said, â€œNow I can see the sunrise every day!â€

Once upon a time, there was a rabbit. The rabbit was very hot. He wanted to find a cool place. So, he went to a river. He saw some birds singing. The rabbit sang with them. He felt happy and cool. The end.

A mom and her little child were walking in the park one day. The child looked at the birds in the trees and asked his mom why they could fly. The mom told him that birds were made to fly. She then asked the child if he wanted to try. The child looked unsure, so the mom decided to make a joke. She said that if he tried he would be like a silly bird. He laughed and decided to give it a try. He ran as fast as he could and spread out his arms. He felt silly because he knew it looked stupid. But then, he felt something strange. He felt like he was flying! He laughed and laughed. He was so happy that he had decided to try. The mom clapped and hugged her child when he returned. She was so excited that he had tried the silly joke. She was so proud that he could take risks, even if they seemed stupid. From that day onward, the child's mom would tell him to try new things even if they seemed silly. Because when he does, he can do things he never imagined!

Once upon a time, there was a mop. The mop was very selfish. It did not like to share. It wanted all the water and soap for itself. One day, the mop met a broom. The broom was very kind. The broom wanted to be friends with the mop. The broom tried to encourage the mop to share. The broom said, "Let's clean together. It will be fun!" The mop thought about it. It wanted to have a friend. So, the mop decided to share with the broom. They cleaned the floor together. They had a lot of fun. The mop learned that sharing is good. The mop was not selfish anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a girl called Lucy. She was only three years old but she was very brave. One day, Lucy noticed a parade going past her house. She was very excited and wanted to join in. When Lucy asked her mom if she could take part in the parade, her mom said, "No, it's too expensive. We can't afford it." Lucy was very sad and she decided to hide so that she wouldnâ€™t be seen. She found a cheap box and got in it. Moments later, she heard music playing. She peeked out of the box and saw that the parade was still going! Lucy was so excited and she quickly rushed out of the box to join in the parade. She had the best time and was having so much fun! When the parade was over and Lucy returned home, she asked her mom, "Can I join the parade again next year?" Her mom laughed and said, "Yes, but only if you donâ€™t hide this time!"

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a shy cat named Mittens. One day, Lily wanted to call her grandma on the telephone. She picked up the phone and dialed the number, but Mittens was scared of the loud ringing sound. "Meow, meow," said Mittens. "It's okay, Mittens," said Lily. "The phone won't hurt you." Suddenly, the phone rang loudly and Lily answered it. "Hello?" she said. "Hi, Lily! It's Grandma!" said a voice on the other end. "Zoom, zoom!" said Lily, excitedly. "Grandma is coming to visit us!" Mittens looked up at Lily and meowed happily. They both couldn't wait for Grandma to arrive.

One day, a polite cat named Kitty went to the store. She wanted to buy butter to make a cake. She walked very fast because she was in a hurry. Kitty liked to speed when she was excited. At the store, Kitty met a dog named Doggy. Doggy was also looking for butter. They both found one big butter. Doggy said, "Can I have the butter, please?" Kitty said, "Yes, you can have it." They both smiled and were happy. Kitty and Doggy decided to make the cake together. They went to Kitty's house and made a big, yummy cake. They shared the cake with their friends. Everyone loved the cake and had a fun time. Kitty and Doggy became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a gray cat named Mittens. One day, Lily wanted to read a book about animals, but she didn't have one. So, she went to her friend's house to borrow a book. When Lily got to her friend's house, she knocked on the door. Her friend answered and Lily asked if she could borrow a book. Her friend said yes and gave her a book about animals. Lily was so happy! As she was walking home, Lily saw a bird with a broken wing. She wanted to help the bird so she used her brain to think of a way to help it. She decided to bring the bird to her mom who knew how to take care of animals. Her mom helped the bird and it flew away happily. Lily was proud of herself for using her brain to help the bird.

Once upon a time, in a wide green park, there lived a tiny insect named Buzz. Buzz was a happy insect who loved to play and make new friends. One sunny day, Buzz found a shiny ring on the ground. The ring was very pretty, and Buzz wanted to keep it. Buzz showed the ring to his friend, Hop, the bunny. Hop told Buzz that the ring belonged to someone else, and they should try to find the owner. But Buzz did not want to give the ring back. He thought it would make him happy to keep it. Hop and Buzz went on a search to find the owner of the ring. They asked all their friends if they had lost a ring. Finally, they found the owner, a little girl who was very sad because she lost her ring. When Buzz gave the ring back to the girl, she was so happy, and she thanked Buzz and Hop. Buzz felt good inside for doing the right thing. The moral of the story is that it is better to be honest and kind, rather than keeping something that does not belong to you.

John was excited! Today he was going to the park. His mom packed a cake in the car, and John had a very comfortable seat as they drove. When they arrived at the park, John saw lots of people playing and having fun. The sun was shining and the warm air smelled sweet. John and his mom did all kind of fun things- they ran, jumped and played tag. Eventually, they sat down in the grass to eat the cake and it was delicious. John had the best time at the park. But, after a few hours it was time to go home. John was sad but happy to be so comfortable in the car for the drive home. John smiled as he thought about his fun day at the park!

Once upon a time, there was a playful dog named Max. Max loved to run and jump and play with his toys. One day, he saw a driver in a big truck drive by. Max wondered where the driver was going. Later that day, Max was feeling tired. He yawned and decided to sleep in his cozy bed. As he was dozing off, he heard a loud noise outside. It was the same big truck from earlier! Max remembered the driver and wondered if he was lost. Max got up and ran outside to greet the driver. The driver was very happy to see Max and thanked him for being so friendly. Max wagged his tail and felt happy that he could help the driver find his way. From then on, Max would always wag his tail when he saw the driver in his big truck.

Once upon a time, there was a giraffe named Jerry. Jerry was very stubborn and never wanted to listen to anyone. One day, Jerry's friend, a monkey named Max, noticed that Jerry had a big knot on his neck. Max asked Jerry if he was okay, but Jerry said he was fine. Max knew that Jerry needed help, so he went to talk to the other animals in the jungle. They all agreed that they needed to discuss how to help Jerry. They decided to ask Jerry again if he was okay and to tell him that he needed to see a doctor. Jerry finally listened and went to see the doctor. The doctor said that Jerry had a knot in his neck because he was being too stubborn and not listening to his body. Jerry learned that it's important to listen to others and take care of yourself. From that day on, Jerry was a better listener and a healthier giraffe.

One day, little Jack went to the market. As he walked along, he noticed something in the corner. It was very peculiar and it seemed to sparkle. He decided to examine it more closely. As he examined it, Jack could see it was something special and quite original. As he picked it up, Jack heard a voice. "Don't touch that," said the old man nearby. Jack obeyed and put the object back down. He was very curious and eager to know what it was, but he decided to listen to the old man. The old man then told Jack that one day he'd know what the sparkling object was. The next week, Jack and his family went to the same market. Jack walked over to the corner, and found a golden crown sparkling in the corner. He immediately remembered the old man's words and realised this was the object he was examining. Jack was delighted and surprised that the old man had foreshadowed the discovery of the golden crown. He wore it proudly, feeling like a true king, and presented the original object to his family.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing in her backyard, but it was a bit dull. One day, her friend Timmy came over to play. "Let's play hide and seek!" said Timmy. Lily was excited and they started to play. But then, Timmy found a butterfly and he wanted to catch it. "Wait, Timmy! We shouldn't catch the butterfly. It's not nice to hurt it," said Lily. "Oh, I didn't mind. I just wanted to see it up close," replied Timmy. Lily explained that we should always be kind to animals and respect them. Timmy realized his mistake and they continued to play together, having fun without hurting anyone. The end.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a boy named Tim. Tim had a toy whip. He liked to play with his whip every day. But sometimes, he felt helpless because he did not know how to use it well. One day, Tim met a kind man. The man saw Tim with his whip and wanted to help him. He said, "I can teach you how to improve and use your whip better." Tim was happy and said, "Yes, please!" The kind man showed Tim how to hold the whip and move it. Tim practiced and practiced. Soon, he was not helpless anymore. He knew how to use his whip very well. Tim was happy and thanked the kind man. And from that day on, Tim played with his whip and had lots of fun.

Once there was a little girl named Lucy. She went walking with her mom one day and they found an orange glove on the ground. "Mommy," Lucy said, "can I keep the glove?" "I suppose so," replied her mom. Lucy was so happy and hugged the glove tightly. When she got home, she put it on her hand and started walking around. Suddenly she heard a sound. "What is that noise?" Lucy asked. "It's the fire station's bell," her mom said. "It's ringing to tell the firefighters that someone needs help." Lucy looked at her orange glove and smiled. She knew that if she was ever in danger she could ring the glove, and the firefighters would come to help her.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play hide-and-seek with her friends. One day, Lily was hiding behind a curtain and she saw a butterfly. She tried to catch it, but it flew away. Later that day, Lily's mom asked her to clean up her toys. Lily wanted to do it by herself because she wanted to be independent. She surrendered and asked for help when she realized it was too hard. At night, Lily's mom read her a bedtime story about a brave knight. The knight was very independent and fought in battles. But one day, he had to surrender to the enemy. Lily asked her mom what surrender meant and her mom explained that sometimes it's okay to ask for help and give up when things are too hard.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a rag that she loved to play with. One day, Lily was playing hide and seek with her friend, Timmy. Timmy said, "Lily, I'm going to peek and find you!" Lily giggled and said, "No peeking, Timmy!" Suddenly, Lily's rag fell on the ground and Timmy accidentally stepped on it. "Oh no, my rag!" cried Lily. Timmy felt bad and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. Let me be graceful and help you pick it up." Lily smiled and said, "Thank you, Timmy. You're a good friend."

Once upon a time there was a poor man. He wanted a better life, but nothing seemed to happen. One day he went for a walk. He saw a tip next to a tree. He was very excited and started to pick it up. Suddenly, something very good happened! The tip was filled with money! The man was overjoyed and ran home to tell his family.
